14.2.9. Boiler Blow down Operation
Boiler blow down is defined as to blow the feed water from the boiler drum whether it is
single or bio drum. The purpose of the blow down operation is to removing the dissolved
solids deposited in the boiler feed water. This non removal of dissolved solids will
develop scale formation inside the boiler tube and lead to slow down the process of heat
transfer and tube failure. Existing boilers TDS design limit is < 1500 ppm and regular
sample LAB RECEIPT IS 120 ppm to 225 ppm.
Centum (MOI) and field co-ordination during boiler blow down
1. Before operating the blow down, boiler drum level set point is to be raised from 50
to 60%.This is to avoid drum level swing while operating blow down whether it is
single or bio drum. Drum level will be compensated as feed water controller
cascading with feed water controller.
2. Keep the contracts working personnel away from boiler area to avoid falling of hot
water from vent line.
3. Cross check the field drum level transmitter with DCS drum level transmitter.
4. Suggest field personnel to open the blow down valve slowly to avoid any drum level
fluctuations.
5. Operate the blow down for at least 5 minutes by maintaining drum level above 50%.
6. Hot blow down water should be diluted with service water while discharging in to
open sewer.
7. Field operator should watch the drum level field gauge continuously till the blow
down operation is completed.
8. Suggest field personnel to close the blow down after reaching the drum level to 50%.
9. DCS Engineer should check the drum level is being controlled in cascade mode and
observe for some time till the drum level is steady.
Continuous blow down:
1. Continue to maintain CBD flow to all boilers as per Technical advice. (3% of BFW
flow). Adjust the chemical dosing rate according to sample analysis.
2. Depending on the TDS value CBD can be slightly increased OR can be throttled.
14.3. Procedure for FCCU-II CO Boilers FD Fan change over
FD fan changeover - Turbine drive to Motor drive
 Check for the adequate suction damper opening of the FD fan motor (due to common
signal from the MOI; suction damper openings of the Motor & Turbine drive fans are
normally same).
 Ensure the Instrument air supply to FD fan motor discharge damper is closed. The
storage instrument air cylinder (of the motor discharge damper) to be bled until the
pressure is zero.
 Initiate the ‘CO boiler FD fan West start’ Push Button at MOI. This gives ‘start
permissive’ for the FD fan motor. Simultaneous opening of the discharge damper is
prevented due to isolation of instrument air to FD fan motor discharge damper.
 Start the FD motor from the field.
 Resume the instrument air supply to FD fan motor discharge damper so as to open it
with a note on the furnace pressure and temporary load drop due to excess air. Field
toggle switch can be operated for closing or opening of the Motor FD fan discharge

 After ensuring satisfactory performance of the FD fan motor; operate the field toggle

Shut off the steam to the FD fan Turbine. And open the Turbine casing drains as

 After ensuring satisfactory performance of the FD fan Turbine; operate the field toggle
switch to open the Turbine FD fan discharge damper with a note on the Furnace
pressure and temporary load drop due to excess air. As a further precaution; the
discharge damper can

 Start the AOP of the FD fan Turbine in the field which has to be stopped and placed
on Auto, only after the MOP of the turbine takes over.

Warm up the Turbine.

Close the Instrument Air to FD fan discharge damper.
 Initiate the ‘CO boiler FD fan East start’ PB at MOI.

FD fan turbine steam I/L shut off valve (if functioning) opens wide.
Simultaneous opening of the FD fan turbine discharge damper is prevented due


After FD fan turbine comes into satisfactory rotation; restore air supply to FD

 Close the FD fan motor discharge damper switch (with a note on the Combustion air

 Press the stop Push button in the field for stopping the FD fan Motor and release the
Push button to make it stand by.
14.4. Procedure for FCCU-I CO Boilers FD Fan change over
The following instructions need to be followed for the smooth operation of New CO
Boiler FD Fan auto cut-in change over facility.
 Ensure the following before starting the FD Fan drive.
 FD Fan Turbine inlet VHP steam and outlet LP steam headers are condensate free
before starting FD Fan Turbine.
 FD Fan motor electrical power is available and load clearance is taken from CPP
before starting the FD Fan Motor.
 “HGV Open” indication is ON at MOI.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 117 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.4 Procedure for FCCU-I CO Boilers FD Fan change over

content:
 Keep the Drive Selector Switch on Neutral mode (middle), on Motor field ON/OFF
board.
 During normal operation of FD Fan, one drive is in service and the other is in auto
mode. Keep the Turbine always in service and ensure that VHP steam passing through
the steam SDV. Ensure that FD Fan discharge damper is Open. Turbine ON indication
will appear on Turbine ON/OFF board.
 Keeping the selector switch towards Turbine (South side) indicates that Motor is in
auto mode. Keeping the selector switch towards Motor (North side) indicates that
Turbines is in Auto mode. After the Shell PFI implementation, by default Turbine
should be in service and Motor should be in auto. Ensure that the selector switch is on
Turbine side when Turbine is in service.
 In auto mode, both FD Fan turbine and Motor suction dampers will operate on auto
from MOI with an equal amount of opening.
 FD Fan Motor or Turbine discharge damper will open on its own after auto cut-in
taken place. Discharge damper of Motor or Turbine open command should be given
from field BMS panel for initial startup.
 In case of auto cut-in, FD Fan turbine or Motor discharge damper will open on its own
for the standby auto cut-in drive after auto cut-in takes place and discharge damper
will close on its own for the tripped drive. Suction damper of tripped drive to be made
zero after taking it to manual mode at MOI for restarting.
 Keep the FD Fan Turbine rolling rpm as 250 to 400 when Motor drive is in service.
Ensure that Turbine start permissive and Turbine OFF indications are present on
Turbine ON/OFF board. OFF press button should be released on Turbine ON/OFF
board.
 Start the FD Fan Turbine as follows:
 Ensure FD Fan Turbine inlet VHP steam and outlet LP steam headers are
condensate free before starting FD Fan Turbine.
 Ensure HGV Open (ZSH 4310) is ON at MOI.
 Keep the Drive Selector Switch on Neutral mode (middle), on Motor field
ON/OFF board.
 Keep the suction damper is about 10% open at MOI.
 Keep the FD Fan Turbine drive on rolling around 400 rpm.
 Press “Turbine on” button on Turbine ON/OFF board.
 Steam SDV (SOV 4301) will open and turbine rpm will reach to 1425 rpm
(Normal).
 Ensure that FD Fan turbine discharge damper will open on its own after SDV is
open.
 Ensure the turbine lube oil pressure is normal (0.6 kg/cm2 to 0.8 kg/cm2) and
BCW cooling water inlet and outlet lines are open.
 Combustion air pressures will be adjusted by operating suction damper at MOI.
 FD Fan Motor start up procedure:
 Ensure that FD Fan motor electrical power is available and take load clearance
from CPP before starting the FD Fan Motor.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 118 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.4 Procedure for FCCU-I CO Boilers FD Fan change over

content:
 Ensure HGV Open (ZSH 4301) is ON at MOI.
 Keep the Drive Selector Switch on Neutral mode (middle), on Motor field
ON/OFF board.
 Keep the suction damper about 10% open at MOI.
 Start the FD Fan Motor from motor ON/OFF board.
 Open the Motor discharge damper from the field BMS panel.
 Check the motor ammeter reading, which should be between 25 to 30 Amp.
 Combustion air pressures will be adjusted by operating suction damper at MOI.
FD Fan Turbine to Motor auto cut in procedure:
 Always keep the FD Fan turbine in service and keep motor on auto mode as per shell
PFI recommendations.
 Ensure that “Turbine start permissive” & Turbine off” are healthy on turbine ON/OFF
board, and off push button is released.
 Keeping the selector switch towards Turbine (South side) indicates Motor in auto
mode.
 Ensures FD Fan motor and turbine suction dampers are in auto mode at MOI.
 If FD Fan Turbine trips, Motor will start in auto mode and motor discharge damper
will open on its own.
 FD Fan turbine discharge damper will close on its own after SDV (SOV 4301) gets
closed.
 Isolate the upstream and downstream steam inlet SDV isolation valves.
 Suction damper of FD Fan turbine will made zero at MOI.
FD Fan Motor to Turbine auto cut in procedure:
 Ensure that motor is in-service and discharge damper is open.
 Keep the turbine under rolling through steam SDV (SOV4301).
 Keep the selector switch towards Motor (North side), indicates Turbine in auto mode.
 Ensure that “Turbine start permissive” & “Turbine off” are healthy on turbine
ON/OFF board, and off push button is released.
 Ensure FD Fan motor and turbine suction dampers are in auto mode at MOI.
 If FD Fan motor trips, FD Fan turbine SDV (SOV 4301) will open in auto and
discharge damper will also open at the same time.
 Take the suction damper into manual mode and close the motor suction damper and
ensure the turbine running is smooth.
 Combustion air pressures will be adjusted by operating suction damper at MOI.
14.5. Procedure to operate Boilers at Turndown Capacity in Power Plant
This procedure is to Operate Power Plant Utility and CO Boilers steam loads at turndown
load to avoid low steam generation and oil dripping in the furnace due to low oil pressure
when the boilers run at low steam loads. This PSI gives the information about the turn
down load of each boiler below which boiler should not be operated.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 119 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.5 Procedure to operate Boilers at Turndown Capacity in Power Plant

content:
Power Plant Boilers steam load will be maintained according the steam demand based on
the process thru put. Normally both new and Old FCCU-I & II CO Boilers will be in
service on CO mode as Waste Heat Boiler and there will not be any firing except FCCU-
1 CO Boiler support burner. FCCU-I CO Boiler-9 or 10 will be in service at minimum
steam load around 30 TPH.
Safety Considerations:
a. When boilers are operated at less than turn down capacity, uneven distribution of
flue gas and steam takes place due to low flue gas and steam velocities.
b. Non uniform flow inside the super heater tubes may even cause stagnation of fluid.
c. Mal-distribution of gas flow outside the tubes can lead to overheating of some tubes
compared to others.
Boiler wise turn down steam loads are given below:
CAPACITY TURNDOWN
BOILER FIRING
DESIGN MCR CAPACITY(TPH)
FCCU-2 CO 40 63 CO/LSHS/FG 16
FCCU-1 CO 40 44 CO/LSHS/FG 22
IBH-9 77.5 84.9 LSHS/FG 19.3(Rev-1)
IBH-10 77.5 84.9 LSHS/FG 19.3(Rev-1)
WIL-8 50 54 LSHS/FG 16.2
WHRS 6 3.5 CO --
a. Maintain the Boilers steam loads above the turndown capacity.
b. Stop the Boiler if the Boiler 3zs steam load is below the turndown capacity.
c. Keep close watch in furnace when the boiler is running at turndown steam load and
maintain 2kg/cm2 difference between burner fuel oil and atomizing steam.
14.6. Procedure for minimizing PRDS opening (PRDS Operation)
Need of minimising PRDS opening
This procedure is to minimize opening of pressure reducing stations control valves
(PRDS) for avoiding direct loss of enthalpy due to pressure reduction.
Various steam pressure levels (being used in VR) are mentioned below
a. VHP Steam (36 kg/cm2 & 360 Deg C)
VHP Steam header pressure is being maintained by utility boilers (IBH-9&10,
FCCU-1 CO Boiler & FCCU-2 CO Boiler) and steam generator in CCR.
b. HP steam (28kg/cm2 & 285 Deg C)
HP steam header pressure is being maintained by FCCU-II CO Boiler. There is no
HP steam consumption except for FCCU-II CO Boiler FD Fan and BFW turbines.
Balance is being reduced to MP steam through PRDS. These boilers are used only
when main boilers / HRSGs are shutdown.
c. MP steam (10.5kg/cm2 & 250 Deg C)
MP steam header is being maintained by HRSGs, process MP steam generators in
CDU-I, CDU-II, CDU-III, VBU, FCCU-I, FCCU-II & DHDS-SRU turbines exhausts
and VHP-MP & HP-MP PRDS.
d. LP steam (3.5kg/cm2 & 155 Deg C)
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 120 of 251

8. CCR BFW lube oil turbine MP LP 3
Guidelines for Minimising PRDS opening is as follows:
The following actions to be taken for closing PRDS
a. For closing VHP-MP PRDS
1. VHP to MP PRDS opening can be minimised by ensuring continuous service of
VHP BFW turbine pumps in PP-II and IBH system.
b. For closing MP-LP PRDS
1. PRDS opening can be minimised by running pumps (mentioned in table: 3) on
turbine drive (instead of running the pump on motor drive) until PRDS is
completely closed. The order mentioned in table: 3 can be followed for running
turbine drive.
c. For handling excess MP steam header pressure
1. Excess MP Steam (in case all six GTG/HRSG are in service) can be controlled
by stopping VHP to LP turbines. FCCU-1 CO / IBH-10 boiler FD fan turbine
and MP BFW turbine can be stopped and motor drive can be started.
2. In case of high MP header pressure and VHP-MP PRDS are completely closed,
PP-I/IBH VHP BFW turbines can be stopped (motor drive to be started).
The following actions to be taken for closing PRDS while different units are shut
down:

Reduction in MP Steam consumption = 10T/hr
No effect in demand/consumption of VHP and LP Steam.
Action required:
a) Reduce the PRDS opening preferably VREP-II VHP to MP reducing station, which
in turn will reduce the load on utilities boilers.
b) If VHP to MP PRDS is completely closed, then MP steam header pressure can be

Reduction in MP Steam generation = 5T/hr

b) LP steam requirement can be met by running flushing oil pump (in CDU-III) on
turbine.
c) MP BFW pump in PP-2 and boilers FD fan turbines (OCO, FCCU-1 CO & IBH-
9&10) can be kept in service for meeting LP steam requirement.
When HRSG-I or II trips, i.e, non-availability of 20 T/hr of MP steam
Reduction in MP Steam generation = 20T/hr
No effect on demand / consumption of VHP and LP steam.
Action required:
a) Increase load on VHP steam generating boilers (FCCU-1 CO Boiler, FCCU-2 CO
Boiler, FCCU-I CO Boilers).
b) Ensue availability of VHP BFW pump turbine in IBH & PP-II and run the pump on
turbine.
When HRSG-III,IV,V or VI trips, i.e, non-availability of 50 T/hr of MP steam
Reduction in MP Steam generation = 50T/hr
No effect on demand / consumption of VHP and LP steam.
Action required:
a) Non-availability of HRSG-III/IV/V/VI will require start-up of FCCU-II CO Boiler.
HP steam produced can be reduced to MP steam to meet requirement.
b) MP to LP steam consumption is to be reduced wherever possible.

Note: 1) Stoppage of pressure reducing turbines will not give any relief in steam load.
2) Pressure reducing turbines are to be stopped only when VHP-MP and MP-LP

14.7. PRDS changeover from IBH VHP-MP to VREP-II VHP-MP
To charge PRDS and change over from the PIC 1319 (IBH VHP TO MP) to VREP-II
VHP to MP steam SPC -2301.
Procedure for PRDS changeover from pic 1319 (IBH TO MP) to VREP_II VHP TO
MP:
1. Check the PRDS instrument connections, air leaks if any in the field before making
it ready for charging.
2. Check the control valve upstream and downstream block valves should be in closed
position and stroke check the control valve for correct operation.
3. Initially open the reducing station control valve SPC 2301 VHP TO MP steam
reducing station for 5% which we are going to take into service and observe the rise
in MP steam header pressure. Simultaneously close the PRDS PIC1319 for 5%.To
balance MP Steam header pressure at 10.5kg/cm2 gradually close the PIC 1319
control valve and leave the reducing station control vale SPC 2301 at certain opening
position.
4. BEWARE of control valve operating position Please LOOK UP AND DOWN
whether it is 0 to 100%open OR reverse operation from 100% to 0% Open.
5. After confirming the MP steam header pressure and pressure reducing station
performance which we have taken in to service, then isolate the control valve which
is taken out of service.
14.8. Normal Operation of WHRS
The objective of this Operating manual is to provide procedure for commissioning &
normal Operation of new WHRS (Waste Heat Recovery Steam Generator) between Old
CO Boiler and FGD-II to enable recovery of heat from hot flue gases at Old CO Boiler
outlet to FGD-II.
BACKGROUND:
a. Hot flue gases from FCCU-II are routed to Old CO Boiler through orifice chamber for
steam generation and hot flue gases, downstream of the Old CO Boiler at 320 DegC,
are routed to FGD-II for SPM & Sox removal.
b. It is observed that 6.0tph pf LP steam generated by recovering the heat from outlet
flue gases of Old CO Boiler.
c. Hence, it is proposed to provide WHRS (Waste Heat Recovery Steam Generator)
between Old CO Boiler and FGD-II to generate steam recovery heat from hot flue
gases. In this process, flue gas temperature at inlet of FGD-II will be reduced from
320 Deg C to 200 Deg C.
SCOPE:
a. This procedure is applicable for commissioning and normal operation of WHRS
installed at the outlet of OCO Boiler.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 126 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.8 Normal Operation of WHRS

content:
b. There is no bypass facility for the Waste Heat Recovery System.
c. WHRS is designed for dry run conditions.
d. OCO boiler flue gas outlet will be routed to new WHRS instead of FGD-II. WHRS
outlet will be routed to FGD-II for treatment.
e. Sulphur dew point was calculated as 175 deg C for the design composition of the
flue gas. Therefore, in order to avoid acid dew point corrosion, the evaporator coil
operating temperature has to be at least 10 deg C above Sulphur dew point.
f. Accordingly, it was envisaged that the boiler is to be operated at 10.5 kg/cm2g and
saturation temperature of 185 deg C. The same is to be let down to 3.0/3.5 kg/cm2g
using a Pressure Control Valve.
g. LP steam generated from the system is routed to LP steam header at the OCO boiler
FD fan turbine discharge.
h. HP BFW for the new system is taken from OCO boiler HP BFW provision at the
upstream of BFW control valve.
i. All other utilities requirement for the new system are taken from FCCU-II battery
limit.
WHRS Design Parameters
a. Steam drum outlet pressure: 12.5 Kg/cm2 (g)
b. Steam drum outlet steam temperature: 185 Deg. C
c. Boiler Capacity : 6.1 TPH
d. Chemical Boil out Procedure:
e. Ensure that DM water/BFW connection is made to the boiler and necessary
chemicals are available at site.
f. Following chemicals are required for the chemical boil out (as per boiler supplier):
g. Ion Exchange make ‘Indion – 1462’
h. Ion Exchange make ‘Indion – 2564’
i. Remove one of the safety valves on the steam drum and make arrangements for
injecting the chemical through the safety valve nozzle.
j. Take fresh water (preferably DM water) in the boiler up to 100 mm above the normal
operating water level of the steam drum.
k. Take sample of this water and check pH, hardness and TDS of the same.
l. Add Indion – 1462 chemical into the steam drum through the provision made. After
chemical loading, nozzle to be blinded.
m. Start the boiler by operating the guillotine dampers on WHRS inlet duct and OCO
Boiler outlet to stack ducts such that the flue gas is partially routed to WHRS. Please
ensure that the pressure of the boiler is maintained at 2.0 kg/cm2g
n. Run the boiler on 2 kg/cm2g for 16 to 18 hours.
o. During this period, check pH and TDS of the boiler water after every 4 hours.
p. After 16 to 18 hours, close the guillotine damper towards WHRS and route the flue
gas from OCO Boiler to stack.
q. Drain this boiler water slowly. Take fresh water two times and flush the boiler.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 127 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.8 Normal Operation of WHRS

content:
r. Repeat the procedure with second chemical also.
s. Please note that during this operation, as the flue is partially being routed to WHRS,
FGD will be in operation.
t. Approximately, 72 m 3 of water is required for this process.
WHRS Commissioning Procedure
a. Once the chemical boil out process of the boiler is completed, steam blow out to be
carried out to remove any unwanted particles in the steam line.
b. Fix the PSVs back to steam drum (that was taken out for facilitating chemical boil out).
c. Install all Instrumentation control valves, pressure gauges, temperature guages and
other instruments.
d. Slowly take HP BFW into the system. HP BFW pressure will be reduced to 18 kg/cm2g
from 55 kg/cm 2 g using PRV located at the battery limit.
e. Ensure that the BFW is filled in the economizer tubes and steam drum is filled up to
50% level.
f. Open steam drum vent valve and start up vent valve before introducing flue gas into
the system. This is to remove any entrained air pockets from the system.
g. Slowly introduce flue gas into the system by operating / controlling Old CO Boiler to
stack damper as WHRS inlet damper was stuck up in open position.
h. As hot flue gases are introduced into the system, BFW will heat up and steam will
be generated, pressurizing the system.
i. Close the steam drum vent valve as the pressure reaches 2.0 kg/cm 2 g.
j. Close start up vent valve as the pressure reaches 5-6 kg/cm 2 g.
k. Pressurize the system slowly till 11.0 kg/cm2g (normal operating pressure of steam
drum) in 8 hours from introduction of flue gases.
l. Please ensure that the steam drum level is maintained continuously at 50% level during
this process.
m. Steam generated is to be routed to LP steam header after reducing the pressure using
PCV (from 11.0 kg/cm 2 g to 3.5 kg/cm 2 g).
n. Soot blowers to be operated once in every 12 hours.
o. Blow down to be maintained at 3% of total steam draw rate.
Important precautions to be followed for commissioning of the WHRS:
a. Execution is completed in all aspects and all instruments are installed.
b. It must be ensured that the gauge glasses and other instruments are in good working
order.
c. It must be ensured that all the dampers are in a good working order and their actual
position is indicated correctly.
WHRS system is equipped with the following facilities (P&IDs are attached for ready
reference):
i. 1.5” HP Boiler Feed Water line with PRV, pressure indicator, flow meter, PSV,
pressure gauge, flow control valve and temperature indicator.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 128 of 251

content:
ii. 8” LP steam line with pressure control valve, pressure indicator, pressure gauges,
steam traps and temperature indicators. Pressure gauges and temperature indicators
are provided at the upstream and downstream of pressure control valve. Startup vent
valve with silencer, pressure safety valves with silencer and flow indicator are also
provided.
iii. Steam drum is provided with demister pad, pressure safety valves with silencers,
temperature & pressure indicators, two numbers of level transmitter and gauges
(each) and pressure gauge.
iv. ¾” chemical injection facility exists from OCO Boiler to WHRS steam drum.
v. 1” CBD facility with flow indicator. This facility is routed to exiting OCO CBD drum.
vi. 1” IBD line from steam drum. This is routed to common drain header. Provision exists
for routing the common drain header to CBD line and existing drain trench of OCO
Boiler.
vii. Following sample points with sample coolers are provided. Service water provision
exists for both the sample coolers
1. CBD line
2. LP steam line
viii. Boiler has economizer and evaporator. Feed water enters economizer section and then
steam drum. Temperature indicator exists for monitoring economizer outlet
temperature.
ix. Three soot blowers with 1.5” MP steam are provided. Soot blowing steam line is
provided with pressure gauge, temperature gauge and trap.
x. Pressure indicators, pressure gauges and temperature indicators exists on WHRS
inlet and outlet for monitoring flue gas conditions. Pressure gauge and temperature
indicator are also provided between evaporator and economizer zone for monitoring
flue gas conditions.
xi. Dust collector is provided at the bottom of WHRS to collect the dust particles.
14.9. Normal Operation of BCW Systems:
During the normal operation of cooling water system. The following routine checks
should be made:
 Keep watch on the bearing cooling water system pressure at the discharge of
cooling water pumps. If the pressure falls below 4.0 kg/em2g set pressure alarm will
come in the DCS control room.
 Check that the cooling water pumps are running satisfactorily.
 Also check from the process control rooms that the consumer are not using cooling
water at more than their design rates.
 Keep Watch on the operation of the Cooling water pumps. Regularly check the
lubrication. Gland cooling water. Motor ampere. Vibration or noise on these
machines.
 Maintain a Watch on the water level in the cooling tower stump. If low-level alarm
sounds then find out the reasons at the earliest and take necessary action.
 Regularly Check temperature of supply & return headers. The pitch of the fans can
also be adjusted (after stopping the fan) to control water temperature.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 129 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.9 Normal Operation of BCW Systems:

content:
 Keep a Watch on the operation of cooling tower fans. Check the lubrication.
Bearing temperature and any vibration in the fans.
 The cooling water parameters should be checked regularly through lab result.
Inhibitor dosage in the first start-up will have to be higher than normal to allow for
layer to be formed on pipeline.
 Cooling tower blow down should be operated basing on lab result.
 Check the suspend solids content of return water from side stream filter, Back flush
the filters as per vendor’s requirement & when suspended solid level goes above
maximum acceptable value. The flow through the filter should be kept minimum
commensurate with suspended solid contents of circulating water.
14.9.1. VREP-I BCW System Operation Procedure
Bearing cooling water requirement is met by four pumps 170-PM-5A/B/C and 170/24-
PM-2G2. 170-PM-5A/C are of capacity 350 m 3 /hr at 6 kg/cm 2 pressure and 170/24-
PM-2G2 is of capacity 97 m 3 /hr at 6.5 kg/cm 2 pressure take suction from the sump and
discharge into a 8 inch supply header. This header supplies to all the units. The 8 inch
supply header is provided with a pressure gauge, Temperature Indicator, Pressure
transmitter and flow transmitter. Also a low pressure alarm provided, which sounds at
MOI PP panel when low pressure activates.
There is one more pump 170-PM-5B is available of capacity 275 m3/hr at 3.5 kg/cm2
pressure. This pump cannot be put in service due to low discharge pressure (shut off
pressure 4.5 kg/cm2).
Normally two pumps one of 170-PM-5A/C with combination of 17024-PM-2G2 will
be in service and the second one is available as standby. The following is the VREP-1
BCW requirement.
Unit BCW Consumption (M 3 /Hr)
Crude Distillation Unit-2 42.2
Vacuum Distillation Unit-2 23.6
Bitumen Blowing Unit 19.0
Fluidised Catalytic Cracking Unit-2 16.9
Merox 25.4
Air Compressor 50.0
Power Plant-2 20.0
Off-site pumps/turbines 14.0
PRU & SRU 4.5
Total Consumption 215.6
BCW after serving its purpose, return to cooling tower through a 8 inch header. One 6
inch line from Merox joins to 8 inch header near the battery limit. One more 6 inch
return header from CDU-2 joined to the return header near the tower. A 2 inch tapping
provided in return header for blow down. The return header provided with a sample
point and temperature indicator.
The hot water from various units is cooled in the induced draft cross flow tower. The
tower is designed for a maximum circulating rate 250m 3 /hr. It has two cells each of
capacity 125 m 3 /hr. The hot water from return header distributed to both the shells either
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 130 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.9 Normal Operation of BCW Systems: > 14.9.1 VREP-I BCW System Operation Procedure

content:
side. The distribution of water can be adjusted by the help of butterfly valves (4nos). The
water then distributed through distributing nozzles and falls across the path of air flow.
Packings are provided inside the tower for braking of water particles and increase the
contact time with air for greater cooling. The cooled water is then collected in cooling
tower basin. The basin has two compartments each receiving water from respective
shells. Water from these compartments flows via two channels to BCW sump. A screen
is installed at the inlet of suction of pump for preventing entry of foreign materials to
pump. The suction sump is having over flow and draining facility. The sump is provided
with high and low level alarms, which sound at MOI PP panel. The sump also provided
with a level controller, which controls makeup water flow depending on level.
To supplement the loss of cooling water due to leak, blowdown, evaporation and drift
through cooling tower, raw water is supplied through 2 inch line by raw water pumps
170-PM-02A/B/C/D/E situated at DM-2. Water flow is controlled by control valve,
which get input from sump level controller. By-pass facility of control valve is also
available. In case of failure of make-up water supply, raw water which is stored for CRP
filter back wash in the Back wash raw water tank can be routed. Also DM water can be
used for makeup through filter back wash lines. Usually 80-90% sump level is
maintained.
14.9.2. VREP-I BCW Pump Change Over Procedure
To charge over VREP-I BCW Pumps without interrupting the BCW Supply to Process Units.
The following Procedure to be adopted for VREP-I BCW Pump Change Over:
1. Inform to RSM and concerned units about BCW pump change over.
2. Ensure that a suction and discharge valves of the pump which is going to be kept in
service is in open condition and open the pump bleeder to release the air lock if any
and close the bleeder, inform to DCS in charge.
3. Note the BCW supply header pressure prior to the changeover.
4. Close the pressure switch take off valve then drain the pressure switch point on
BCW supply header.
5. Stand by pump which is in auto mode gets started automatically sensing the BCW
pressure drop to 4.0 kg/cm 2 .
6. Open the pressure switch take off valve and close the pressure switch drain valve on
BCW supply header.
7. Note down the BCW supply header pressure after auto cut in by stand by pump, this
pressure should be more than the pressure noted prior to the start up the stand by
pump.
8. Stop the pump which is in service earlier not and ensure the pump rotating in
reverse direction.
9. Again observe the discharge pressure of the pump that is kept in service.
10. Keep the stand by pump in auto mode and running pump in manual mode and
inform to the DCS in charge.
14.9.3. VREP-II BCW System Operation Procedure
VREP-2 Bearing cooling water requirement is met by two pumps 170-PM-9A/B. 170-
PM-9A/B of capacity 400 m3/hr at 5.5 kg/cm2 pressure take suction from the sump and
discharge into a 12 inch supply header. From the supply header one 3 inch tap-off is taken
for FCCU feed pump and Recirculating cooling water tower. Another 10 inch tap-off
taken for Merox unit and air compressor. There is a 6 inch tie-in between VREP-1 and
VREP-2 BCW supply at Merox unit. After the take of for Merox block the 12 inch header
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 131 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 10 Keep the stand by pump in auto mode and running pump in manual mode and > 14.9.3 VREP-II BCW System Operation Procedure

content:
reduced to 10 inch and supply water to CDU-3. From this 10 inch header one 2 inch tap-
off is taken to cater to VBU feed pump near DM plant. The supply header is provided
with a pressure gauge, Temperature Indicator, Pressure transmitter and flow transmitter.
Also a low pressure alarm provided, which sounds at MOI PP panel when low pressure
activates. Auto cut-in facility of spare pump is also available. At 4.5kg/cm2 auto cut-in
of spare pumps takes place.
Unit BCW Consumption (M3/Hr)
Crude Distillation Unit-3 155.0
Vacuum Distillation Unit-3
Vis Breaker Unit 5.5
Salt water cooling tower
Merox / ATU/SWS 37.0
Air Compressor-VREP-2 130.0
VBU feed pumps 7.0
HVGO (FCCU feed) pumps
Total Consumption 334.5 m 3 /hr
BCW returns form all consumer units as same size of supply headers are connected to a
12 inch header which feeds warm water to cooling tower top. One 3 inch tapping
provided in return header for Side stream filter 170-X-09 of capacity 8 m3/hr. It filters
suspended solids 60 ppm at inlet to 10 ppm at outlet. Feed line to filter is provided with
a flow indicator. One DP indicator is provided across the filter bed. Max allowable DP
is 0.5 kg/cm2. After filtration, this water returns to cooling water sump. Back flushing
facility is provided for the Side stream filter. This is to be back washed once in a day or
if DP across the filter crosses 0.5 kg/cm2 whichever comes earlier. The return header
provided with a pressure gauge, Temperature gauge and temperature indicator. One 8
inch tapping provided from return header for blow down. Blow down water is routed to
surface drain.
The hot water from the various units is cooled in the induced draft cross flow tower. The
tower is designed for a maximum circulating rate 400m3/hr. It has two cells each of
capacity 200 m3/hr. The hot water from return header distributed to both shells on either
side. The distribution of water can be adjusted by help of butterfly valves on individual
distribution line. The water then distributed through distributing nozzles and fall across
the path of air flow. Packing are provided inside the tower for braking of water particle
and increase the contact time with air for greater cooling. The cooled water then collected
in cooling tower basin. The basin has two compartments each receiving water from
respective shell. Water from these compartment flows via two channels to BCW sump.
A double screen is installed at the channel near inlet of sump for preventing entry of
foreign materials to the pump suction. The basins are provided with isolating gate. Each
basin is provided with drain. The suction sump is having over flow and draining facility.
The drain valve operated to remove mud and slim deposit. BCW pumps take suction
from the sump. Suction strainers are provided inside the sump. The sump is provided
with high and low level alarms, which sound at MOI PP panel . The sump also provided
with a level controller, which controls make-up water flow depending on level.
To supplement the loss of cooling water due to leak, blow down, evaporation and drift
through cooling tower, raw water is supplied through 4 inch line by raw water pump 170-
PM-02A/B/D/E situated at DM-2. One flow meter is provided on the makeup water line
to know the makeup. Water flow is controlled by control valve, which gets input from
sump level controller. By-pass facility of control valve also available. In case of failure
of make-up water supply, raw water which is stored for CRP filter back wash in the Back

content:
wash raw water tank can be routed. Also DM water can be used for makeup through filter
back wash water tank lines. Usually 80-90% sump level is maintained.
14.9.4. VREP-II BCW Pump Change Over Procedure
Bearing cooling water supply to rotary equipment of the refinery is continuous in nature
and has to be maintained without any disturbance. To get Reliability and continuous
performance of pumps periodical change over as well as PM checks are required.
The following procedure to adopted while changing the pump
1. Inform to RSM and concerned units about BCW pump changeover.
2. Ensure that a suction and discharge valves of the pump which is going to be kept in
service is in open condition, inform to DCS in charge.
3. Note the BCW supply header pressure prior to the changeover.
4. Close the pressure switch take off valve then drain the pressure switch point on
BCW supply header.
5. Stand by pump which is in auto mode gets started automatically sensing the BCW
pressure drop to 4.5 kg/cm 2 .
6. Open the pressure switch take off valve and close the pressure switch drain valve on
BCW supply header.
7. Note down the BCW supply header pressure after auto cut in of stand by pump, this
pressure should be more than the pressure noted prior to the start up the stand by
pump.
8. Stop the pump which is in service earlier and ensure the pump not rotating in
reverse direction.
9. Again observe the discharge pressure of the pump that is kept in service.
10. Ask DCS in-charge to keep the stand by pump in auto mode and running pump in
manual mode.
14.10. Normal Operation of PA System
HPCL-VR Power and utilities require maximum safety levels due to the chemical
processing industry. Reliable voice communication as well as clear full-coverage
announcements and warnings in potentially explosive environments are the critical
factors here for man, machinery and the environment.
Industronic PA System using in this area and total no. of available PAs are 27 available.
PA operation will do in following cases are
1. Any communication from field to DCS
2. Any communication from DCS to field.
To Operate PA precautions to be followed to maintain quality of Communication
between DCS and Field
1. PA system has base unit in DCS and Outdoor inter come station in field and both
have microphone and loudspeaker
2. To communicate any information from DCS to field, DCS operator use the base
unit of particular earmark outdoor inter com station on base unit available in DCS
area
3. To communicate any information from field to DCS, Field crew use the outdoor
inter come station of PA system available at location, Power & utilities available
outdoor inter come stations are 27 for field communication to DCS. DCS Base
unit has membrane keypad to communicate field locations

information to DCS.
5. Rocker switch is generally in horizontal position by default.
6. Field crew pressing of Rocker switch of outdoor inter come station, DCS operator
will get the LED bulb of Base unit will glow at particular location and receive the
voice through speaker.
7. DCS crew pressing of base unit membrane key board particular switch of
outdoor inter come station, Field crew will get the LED bulb will glow at
particular location outdoor inter com station and receive the voice through
speaker.
8. Base unit connected to 27 locations. Therefore, any PA usage from base unit is
alert the other outdoor inter com stations with LED bulb glow in all outdoor inter
com stations.
9. LED bulb of outdoor inter com station glow means DCS operator is already busy
with other Outdoor inter com station crew. So field crew should wait up to
completion of other outdoor inter com conservation means LED bulb off. Then
start to communicate.
10. By mistake two or more outdoor inter com stations are operating in same time
due to emergency DCS operator will not get the clear voice from anyone. So
before operating rocker switch LED bulb alertness should be confirm.

16.2. Operating Procedures of Critical Equipment:
16.2.1. Operating Procedures for Centrifugal Pumps:
Sl. Activity Procedure
1. Start-up i. Inspect and see if all the mechanical jobs are completed.
ii. Establish cooling water flow where there is such provision.
Also open steam for seal quenching in pumps having such
facilities.
iii. Check oil level in the bearing housing, flushing may be
necessary if oil is dirty or contains some foreign material.
iv. Rotate the shaft by hand to ensure that it is free and coupling is
secure. Coupling guard should be in position and secured
properly.
v. Open suction valve. Ensure that the casing is full of liquid.
Bleed, if necessary, from the bleeder valve.
vi. Energize the motor. Start the pump and check the direction of
rotation. Rectify the direction of rotation if it is not right.
vii. Check the discharge pressure. Bleed if necessary to avoid vapor
locking.
viii. Open the discharge valve slowly. Keep watch on the current
drawn by the motor, if ammeter is provided. In other cases

check at motor control Centre. In some pumps a by-pass has
been provided across the check valve and discharge valve to
keep the idle pump hot. In such pumps, the by-pass valve should
be closed before starting the pump. It should be ensured that
casing of these pumps are heated up sufficiently prior to starting
of the pump to guard against damage of the equipment and
associated piping due to thermal shock.
2. Shutdown a. Close discharge valve fully.
b. Stop the pump
3. Keeping If pump is going to remain as standby and has provision for keeping
pump the pump hot, proceed as follows:
standby.
o Open the valve in the by-pass line across the discharge valve and
check valve.
o The circulation rate should not be so high to cause reverse
rotation of idle pump and also overloading of the running pump.
4. Releasing to If pump is to be prepared for maintenance, proceed as follows:
maintenance o Close suction and discharge valves.
.
o Close valve on check valve by-pass line, if provided.
o Close cooling water to bearing, if provided. Also shut off steam
for seal quenching, if provided.
o Slowly open pump bleeder and drain liquid from pump if the
liquid is very hot allow sufficient time before draining is started.
Ensure that there is no pressure in the pump. Also drain pump
casing.
o Blind suction and discharge and check valve by-pass line and
flare connection if any.
o Cut-off electrical supply to pump motor prior to handling over
for maintenance.
16.2.2. Operating Procedures for FD fans:
Operation of the centrifugal fan should follow the equipment manufacturers
recommended procedures. Verify that the lubrication and cooling water systems are
clean and free of contamination and that all control systems function properly. Verify
that the shutdown system of the blower/fan is according to design. Verify that the
blower/fan rotate freely.
Sl. Activity Procedure
1. Start-up /  Open and suction and discharge dampers.
Normal  Check the lubricating oil & grease system on all bearings.
operation  Log all temperatures, pressures, levels, flows.

Adjust cooling water flows to compensate for changes in inlet water
temperature or ambient temperature.

Start the Blower drive.

Listen for any unusual noise while the machine is operating. These
should be investigated immediately
2. Shutdown a. Stop the blower drive.
b. Start the oil pump to lubricate the bearing till required.
c. Close the discharge valve.

16.3. Operating Procedures of WHRS:
16.3.1 PRELIMINARY CHECK
Before commencing pressure increase make sure that every auxiliary / equipment is
in a satisfactory working order and all checks have been made. 100 mm water must
be indicated in the water gauge glasses. The water level must be checked by opening
and closing the drain valves. When the water moves slowly in the glasses, check why
this is so. All blow down and drain valves must be closed.
16.3.2 EXPANSION OF PRESSURE PARTS
Make sure that all suspension ties are stressed and that the weight is uniformly
distributed over all suspension points for top supported units.
All tube walls must be gas tight, and the interspaces between the boiler headers and
tubes must, where necessary, be sealed with non-asbestos rope.
Prior to each startup of suspended boilers it is necessary to check whether or not there
is any foreign matter in the part expanding downward. Serious complications may
result from obstructions to expansion.
Similarly, for bottom supported boilers, free expansion to be allowed for steam drum
& top headers. When the boiler is generating at full capacity & pressure the actual
vertical and lateral expansions should be recorded and compared with anticipated
expansion diagram enclosed. This could be done by marking cold & hot position with
marker on a near stationary structural member. For anticipated expansion refer to
fixture holder drawing.
After modifications and repairs have been made the engineer in-charge should be
responsible for checking, prior to re-commissioning, if sufficient margins are given
for expansion.
At intervals of 1 to 2 months it is necessary to check the extent of the expansions
under equal operating conditions. Modifications must be communicated to the station
superintendent.
16.3.3 PRECAUTIONS PRIOR TO INLET OF CO BOILER GAS
Any scaffolding timber must be removed from the Boiler and from the gas passes,
especially where the gas temperature reaches the ignition temperature of the timber.
As a matter of fact, glowing ash could cause the ignition of highly explosive gases
obtained by combustion with low firing rate during boil out.
The pipe line connections must be made so that all vent and drain valves can be
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:16 Rev. No.2 Page 151 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS: > 16.3.3 PRECAUTIONS PRIOR TO INLET OF CO BOILER GAS

content:
operated efficiently without any risk for the erection or operating personnel.
It must be ensured that the gauge glasses and other instruments indicating the water
level are in good working order.
It must be safeguarded that all dampers are in a good working order and
have been mounted in such a way that their actual position is indicated correctly.
Make sure that the unit is filled with water and chemical agents in accordance with
the recommended practice.
When the water gauge glass is dirty, close the water shut-off cock and open the steam
shut-off cock. By opening the drain valve for a short moment it is possible to purge
the gauge glass. As soon as the indicating instrument is clean, the drain valve must
be closed and the water shut-off cock must be opened.
With the rising boiler pressure observe the movement of the pressure gauge to make
sure that the instrument functions correctly. Carefully check the manhole and hand-
hole cover joints. In case of necessity repair them.
Check the whole boiler for gas and steam leakages. On the second day slowly increase
the pressure 30 per cent of what it is in normal operation; maintain this pressure
during the whole day while testing the mountings, such as water level indicators,
remote water level indicators etc., with steam, preparing them for operation and then
closing and draining them.
After the pressure had been lowered again during the night, it shall be brought, during
the third day, to the boil out pressure which is 50 per cent of the normal working
pressure; this pressure shall be maintained during the whole day in order to complete
boil out.
After boil out it is advisable to purge the all lines in order to make sure that no foreign
matter is carried along the steam line when starting up the plant. As soon as the boiler
is cold, open the vents and drains in order to empty the boiler and the Economizer.
Any sludge accumulated in the drum and headers must be removed. Thereupon close
all drains and fill the boiler with clean water. When water is showing in the water
gauges, continue the filling process slowly until drum is completely filled. Then
empty the boiler.
Before enter gases removed all foreign material inside the boiler. After cleaning inlet
damper open partially.
16.3.4 WATER LEVEL INDICATORS
Check the water gauges in accordance with the following method.
1) Partially open the discharge valve for half a minute so that glass and instrument
slowly reach the maximum temperature; then open the discharge valve wide.
When the discharge valve is being opened quickly, the glass may break.
2) Check the steam connection by closing the water valve so that steam will leave
through gauge glass and drain.
3) Open the steam valve.
4) Check the water connection by closing the steam valve and opening the water
valve. Close the water valve again when water is flowing.
5) Open the steam valve.
6) Close the drain valve when the water returns immediately to its correct level in the
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:16 Rev. No.2 Page 152 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS: > 16.3.4 WATER LEVEL INDICATORS

content:
gauge glass. When the water moves slowly, this indicates that the steam or water
connection is partially obstructed and that the water level indicator does not
function properly. The service engineer must convince himself that each boiler
operator knows how to carry out the water level check.
7) Contamination of the water gauge glasses is possible during boil out. Before
commissioning boiler, it is necessary to dismantle and clean these glasses; the
mica on flat glasses must be replaced.
Then the responsibility for feeding can be taken over by the boiler operator. This
precautionary measure is particularly necessary when starting up new plants, because
the operators are generally not yet familiar with the plant and other duties deserving
their attention.
As soon as the water level is falling, feed a continuous water flow into the boiler.
Avoid feed interruption, which will cause unwanted temperature changes in the
Economiser.
Check the high and low water alarm by raising and lowering the boiler water level.
Note the water level at which the alarm is given.
After the boiler has been fed for five minutes, close the Economiser bypass valve, if
existing. A new unit should be allowed to steam at low load for some time in order to
check the reliability and to give the operator the opportunity of becoming acquainted
with the checks. Use should be made of this trial period for checking if all instruments
and the complete equipment of the boiler plant are in a satisfactory working order.
When the boiler has taken up steam generation, rules should be established for the
operating conditions existing; such rules can then be used as a guide for safe and
economic operation. Temperature and draught shall be measured exactly during a
period of some hours to check if they correspond to the values indicated so that
changes are generally not necessary. Minor differences are of little importance.
When the draught loss in the boiler increases considerably, inspect the heating
surfaces occasionally in order to find and remove deposits, if any, in the flue gas
passes. It is advisable to commence keeping records about fuel consumption, steam
quantity, flue gas analyses, gas temperatures, draught, fuel analyses, operating hours,
average data etc. as soon as possible, because such records give an idea of measures
which are valuable for finding and eliminating faults.
16.3.5 RAISING THE PRESSURE
During initial pressure raising for cold start the pressure should be raised slowly to
avoid thermal shock on boiler parts to refractory. Please refer the pressure-raising
curve given next to this page.

17.3 Avoiding Deviations & Plant Upsets:
To avoid Deviations and Plant upsets precautionary steps to be taken to the extent
possible. These steps may include

Routine bi-hourly field rounds.

100% compliance of DRJ.

Releasing equipment to PM checks.

Releasing equipment to PET RP.

Tracking the system through DSI Reports.
17.3.1Routine bi-hourly field rounds:
Employee has to take a routine bi-hourly field round and observe in a detailed way
about the condition of each equipment. Readings are to be noted in Turnover book. If
any of the reading is abnormal then it is to be highlighted immediately for corrective
action. Besides reading also observe the physical condition related to performance and
safety. Eg lube oil dripping from soot blowers, poor housekeeping etc.
17.3.2100% compliance of DRJ:
Daily Routine Jobs (DRJs) related to operation are to be followed with 100%
compliance as applicable to shift, day, week and month. Deviations in DRJs are to be
addressed immediately for system improvement.
17.3.3 Releasing equipment to PM checks:
Release of Rotary and Electrical equipment is to be followed as per PM check schedule.
The hidden problems in equipment will be highlighted and corrected through this
Preventive Maintenance schedule concept. Otherwise it may lead to damage of
equipment and at times loss of production also. Deviations in PM checks are to be
addressed and tracked for system improvement.
17.3.4 Releasing equipment to PET RP:
Plant Equipment Turnaround Release Plan is to be followed for all static critical
equipment listed in PET RP book. Release Plan is to be made and followed for
Inspection and Maintenance of the equipment. Deviations in PM checks are to be
addressed and tracked for system improvement.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:17 Rev. No.2 Page 160 of 251

17.3.5 Tracking the system through DSI Reports:
Various DSI (Departmental Standing Instructions) Reports are to be released as per
schedule (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be
addressed and tracked for system improvement.

content:
18.1. Automatic Emergency Shutdowns:
Emergencies that occur in power plant units must be recognized and acted upon
immediately. The operators and supervisory personnel should carefully study in advance,
and become thoroughly familiar with, the proper steps to be taken in such situations.
Some of the emergency conditions described here will not only resulting in unit shut
down, but also unsafe handling can lead to serious damage to the equipment and
personnel.
In general, the objective of the emergency procedures is to avoid damage to the
equipment and personnel.
In addition, specific rules cannot be made to cover all situations that might arise.
Therefore, the following procedures cover only those emergency situations that are the
most common and, for the most part, only the immediate steps to be taken are listed. It
should be noted that the procedures outlined here concentrate on the power plant section.
18.2. Emergency Shutdown Procedures:
In case of emergency, immediate shut down is required. Certain emergencies are
identified and the procedures are explained as follows.
1. FCCU-II CO Boiler Trip
2. FCCU-I CO Boiler Trip
3. Electrical power failure
4. Boiler tube leak
18.2.1. Emergency shutdown procedure when FCCU-II CO Boiler trip:
1. Press emergency trip PB.
2. Immediately close oil valve.
3. Immediately close gas valve.
4. Immediately divert CO gas to stack.
5. Scavenge the Burner, Manually if possible.
6. If you want to put in service in a short time then purge the Boiler for few minutes and
start again.
18.2.2. Emergency shutdown procedure when FCCU-I CO Boiler trip:
1. Press emergency trip PB.
2. Immediately close oil valve.
3. Immediately close gas valve.
4. Immediately divert CO gas to stack.
5. Scavenge the Burner, Manually if possible.
6. If you want to put in service in a short time then purge the Boiler for few minutes and
start again.
18.2.3. Emergency shutdown procedure when Electrical power failure:
1. Inform the failure to the S/E, Mgr. YSF.
2. After power failure all electrical driven pumps, blowers, agitators will be tripped.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:19 Rev. No.2 Page 165 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > 18.2 Emergency Shutdown Procedures: > 18.2.3 Emergency shutdown procedure when Electrical power failure:

content:
3. But PLC and PC will work on UPS.
So after power failure close Plant Inlet battery limit Valve Isolate all beds in auto or
manual. Close Out let battery limit Valve.
4. After restoration of Electrical power start all rotary equipment like Pump, blower etc.
and put plant in service, as per the procedure explained in chapter-15
18.2.4. Emergency shutdown procedure when Boiler Tube leak ( main bank, economizer
and super heater):
1. If leak is minor, report to manager and inspection and maintenance to take decision
to stop the boiler.
2. If Leak is heavy, inform Shift foreman and MOI engineer and stop the Boiler as
per procedure.
3. If Leak is very heavy stop the boiler by operating ESD push button.
18.3 EMERGENCY HANDLING PROCEDURE
18.3.1 Low Water Level
Possible Causes
1. Control failure
2. Boiler Feed Pump failure
3. Tube leak
4. Operator's error
Action to be taken
1. Compare indication, if the water level falls out of sight in the water gauge, due to failure
of the feed water supply or neglect of the operator, except in cases of momentary
fluctuations that might occur with extra ordinary changes in load, appropriate action
should be taken at once to trip the fuel. Any decision to continue to operate, even if
only for a short time at a reduced rating, would have to be made by someone in authority
who is thoroughly familiar with the circumstances that led to the emergency and
positively certain that the water level can be restored immediately without damaging
the boiler. In the absence of such a decision:
2. Gas inlet damper closed immediately
3. Open all inspection and access door.
4. Shut off all steam discharged from the unit (trip turbine, steam driven auxiliaries, etc.)
open start up vent valve.
5. Simultaneously, if feed water has become available and the operator is assured that no
pressure parts have been damaged, gradually reduce (never increase) the flow of feed
water to the boiler by manual regulation. The purpose of handling the feed in this
manner is to avoid quenching pressure parts with relatively cold water by raising the
water level and on the other hand to avoid exposing additional metal. The feed water
regulating valve should be closed completely when all steam flow from the unit has
ceased.
6. Maintain a high air flow at first to hasten the cooling process.
7. If pressure parts damage is suspected, reduce the steam pressure gradually by opening
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:19 Rev. No.2 Page 166 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > 18.3 EMERGENCY HANDLING PROCEDURE > 18.3.1 Low Water Level

content:
the start-up drain. Open the drum vents when the pressure drops below 25 psig. As the
boiler cools reduce the air flow. Shut down the damper as soon as the unit is cool enough
for a man to enter. Drain the boiler when the drum metal temperature is 200 0 F following
normal draining procedures. Determine the cause of low water and examine the boiler
for the effects of possible overheating such as leaks and distortion of pressure parts.
18.3.2 High Water Level
WATER IMPINGEMENT MAY CAUSE TURBINE DAMAGE
Possible Causes
1. Feed water control malfunction.
2. Operator's error.
Action to be taken
Abnormally high water level should be avoided as it may lead to carryover and even
priming. If the water level rises above the recommended normal operating range proceed
as follows:
1. Reduce the water level immediately by opening the intermittent blow down valves.
2. Reduce the steam rate, if necessary, and place feed water control on Manual & reduce
water flow rate.
3. If priming should occur, as indicated by rapid fluctuations in outlet steam temperature,
proceed as follows:
4. Reduce the steaming rate.
5. If the water level is abnormally high, reduce the level by opening the intermittent blow
down valves and place feed water control on manual.
6. Investigate the water condition (alkalinity and solids) and other contaminants.
7. Investigate the condition of the drum internals as soon as an opportunity is afforded.
18.3.3 TUBE LEAKS INDICATION OF TUBE LEAKS
Indication of Tube leaks
1. Feed water flow greater than steam flow.
2. Hissing noise from inside boiler.
3. Wetted insulation of water running down casing with no apparent cause.
4. Excessive make-up.
Action to be taken
1. Water wall tube leaks
1. The action taken is dependent on size and location of tube leak. A large leak may
extinguish fire or cause loss of drum level. A medium size leak may force an immediate
shut down. A small leak may allow continued operation after considering its
consequences on continued operation.
2. If at all possible, locate the leak.
3. In the event of failure of one or more water carrying tubes, the best method of shutting
down the unit will be dictated by the size of the failure, the ability to maintain normal
water level and the demands for the service of the unit.
4. The following instructions regarding tube failures are of a general nature. It must be
understood that conditions may arise which will require exercise of judgement by the
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:19 Rev. No.2 Page 167 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > WATER IMPINGEMENT MAY CAUSE TURBINE DAMAGE > 18.3.3 TUBE LEAKS INDICATION OF TUBE LEAKS

content:
operators.
5. In case of a leak or tube failure, which does not involve a serious drain on the feed
water supply, the water level should be maintained, and the unit taken out of service in
the normal manner. Refer to normal shutdown procedures.
6. If the tube failure results in a loss of water so great that the water level cannot be
maintained with the feed water supply available, use the method outlined below :
 Close immediately inlet Flue gas
damper.
 Shut off feed water to the boiler.
 Open all inspection and accesses door.
7. After the unit has cooled enough to permit a man to enter it, make a thorough inspection
of pressure parts of any indication of damage resulting from loss of water level. After
the necessary repairs have been made, apply a hydrostatic test and obtain the approval
of the proper authorities before putting the unit back in service.
18.3.4 Economizer Tubes and Evaporator tube
a) An economizer or evaporator tube leak can be detected by sound and/or increased make
up water requirements. The leak should be investigated at the earliest possible time and
unit shutdown in a normal manner.
b) Water leaks in the economizer or evaporator can cause considerable erosion damage to
adjacent tubes. Water carried over from an economiser tube leak may cause plugging of
hoppers. Any decision to continue operation with known leaks should be made with this
in mind.
18.3.5 Safety Precautions to be followed in any Boiler
The handling and burning of any fuel is potentially hazardous. Some fuels ignite more
readily than others. Safe handling and operation demands knowledge of the
characteristics of the fuel and careful observance of necessary precautions.
1) The raising and lowering of steam parameters should be restricted to the value given
in the starting diagram. Exceeding these values will result in reduced fatigue life of
pressure part.
2) In the case of tube failure which can be identified by hearing the noise in the boiler
gallery and cross checked by difference in steam and water flow, gas and steam
temperature, the boiler should be shut-down at the earliest by regular procedure for
maintenance work, otherwise large number of tubes may fail due to steam erosion
and impingement.
3) Boiler washing when the unit is shutdown can be done effectively by using hot water.
Immediately after washing, the surfaces should be dried by firing boiler at low rate.
4) Always use deaerated water for boiler feeding. Feed water temperature must also be
maintained at the highest level possible either with the help of feed water heaters or
heating the water in the feed tank.
5) Low feed water temperature obtained during low load operation will result in
external corrosion of economizer.
6) Carryover of salts in steam occurs either due to mechanical or vapor carry over.
Efficient drum internals can only reduce mechanical carry over. Silica is always
carried over in vapor form. Continuous monitoring of sodium & silica in steam is
desirable.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:19 Rev. No.2 Page 168 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > WATER IMPINGEMENT MAY CAUSE TURBINE DAMAGE > 18.3.5 Safety Precautions to be followed in any Boiler

content:
7) Before operating the boiler, ensure complete knowledge of water chemistry.
8) The steam drum should not normally be filled beyond the point when water is
showing in the bottom of the gauge glass. This is to allow for the swell on heating
and to reduce any blowing down from this cause to a minimum.
9) Raising pressure on boilers, a technique of continuous firing should be developed if
possible, since intermittent firing may result in re condensation in the tube loops.
10) The blow down or drain valves which allow the hot water or steam to flow under
pressure in a relatively cold line shall be opened slowly. For low pressures &
temperatures relatively less time is required. When the water level cannot be
observed by the person operating the blow down valves, a helper shall be stationed
so that he can observe the water level & can simultaneously give the instructions for
opening or closing the blow down valves in accordance with the requirements.
18.4 Re-Startup after Emergency Shutdowns:
During emergency some equipment will trip in auto mode for safety reason. Some of the
equipment have to be shutdown manually when condition is beyond control and
normalcy is not coming immediately. In such situations careful coordination from field
to MOI and to other units and YSF is essential. There may be many examples of
emergency. Few of them are

Boiler trip

BCW ID Pump or Fan trip

PRDS controller failure
After rectification of emergency problem the affected equipment can be put back in
service as per standard procedure.
Shift Event Report (SER) is to be filled for the emergency faced. Lessons learnt are to be
captured for system improvement.

content:
19.1. Preparations and readiness for planned shutdown:
If shutdown is necessary due to failure of feed water supply, stop firing as quickly as
possible. Open super heater drains and shut off main valve. If shutdown is happened
due to failure of one or more steam generating tubes, follow the normal shutdown
procedure. Always check for the possibility of maintaining normal drum level. If it is
not possible to maintain water level, damp or extinguish fires as quickly as possible.
Regardless of the cause of shutdown, allow the steam generating unit sufficient time to
cool down after the fire is out and unit is shut off from the steam line before circulating
cold air or water. Cold air may be admitted to furnace after the pressure has dropped to
zero and after the refractory surfaces have ceased to show any color.
19.2. Checklists to be followed for S/D.
The Check list to be followed during Shut down is mentioned in in Annexure-II which
is attached at the end of Manual.
19.3. Detailed procedure for safe shut down of Boiler
1. Soot blowing should be done at normal load, if possible.
2. Slowly change over from gas to oil firing (if on dual firing).
3. Place boiler on manual control.
4. Slowly reduce the load.
5. Shut-down the Burner.
6. Close the oil valve.
7. Scavenge the Burner and pull out the burner, after pilot flame is cut off.
8. Open the startup vent valve and super heater outlet drain valve few threads
immediately after removing oil fire.
9. Close the main stop valve.
10. Keep the FD fan running until the furnace cooled up to 50 to 60 Deg.C
11. This is contracted to air to be allowed after boiler pressure reaches low.
12. Maintain normal water level during cooling period.
13. When the drum pressure reaches 20# open the vent valve.
14. Close the main FO, FG, atomizing steam and FW isolation valve.
15. When the temperature drops below 100 Deg.C operate MBD.
19.4. Detailed procedure for safe shut down of PRDS.
 Close de-super heater water valve.
 Coordinate with MOI Engineer to shift steam distribution to another PRDS.
 Close PRDS upstream and downstream valves slowly in coordination with MOI.
19.5. Detailed procedure for safe shut down of BCW Systems
 Maintain BCW Sump level low to accommodate BCW return
 Stop BCW Pumps after informing to YSF and MOI Engineer and close pump suction
and discharge valves
 Close BCW supply and return lines at Battery limit.
 Stop chemical dosing to BCW Sump
 Stop ID fans.
19.6. Detailed Boiler shutdown Precautions pertaining to Steam scavenging of FO
burners & Yard air to Boiler Peep hole glass
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:19 Rev. No.2 Page 173 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 3 Place boiler on manual control. > 19.6 Detailed Boiler shutdown Precautions pertaining to Steam scavenging of FO

content:
1. Precaution while shutting down the burner/ Boiler to prevent:
1. Plugging of the Oil gun.
2. Damage of the Ignitor due to deposition of heavy oil.
3. Accumulation of water inside furnace.
2. Plugging of the Oil gun : Some times, the Fuel Oil gun is not scavenged immediately
after stopping of the burner. The fuel oil inside the burner pipe & tip solidifies. These
solidified oil is very difficult to clean / clear the burner holes. The Performance of
the burner reduces due to partial cleaning.
3. Inorder to avoid ‘ Plugging of the Oil gun after stopping the burner, the instruction
mentioned in the S. No: 5.1 & 5.3 to be followed.
4. Damage of the Ignitor due to deposition of heavy oil: Also, on few occasions, steam
scavenging of the Fuel Oil burners is attempted with out the Pilot flame where
Automatic Ignition Cycle ON provision is not available during scavenging. This
causes the Fuel oil traces to fall on the Ignitor tip and the surrounding, affecting the
Ignitor’s performance.
5. Inorder to avoid the Fuel Oil traces deposition on the Boiler Ignitor while scavenging
the Fuel Oil burners, the instructions mentioned in S. Nos : 2 & 3 are to be followed
6. Accumulation of water inside furnace: Passing of atomising steam into the furnace
may cause accumulation of water due to condensation, inside of the furnace leading
to start up delay.
Inorder to avoid water accumulation in the furnace through the objectionable water
carry over in the yard air meant for the peep hole glass of the Boiler/ Soot blowers
when the Boiler is shutdown; instructions mentioned in the S. No: 5.4 & 5.5 to be
followed.
7. The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner

Fuel Oil burners have to be scavenged immediately after removing the Oil fires
while shutting down the boiler or even while stopping the burner.

If the fuel oil burner is not scavenged immediately after stopping, the fuel oil may
clog the burner pipe and burner tip as well. If the gas burner kept ‘ON’ with out
scavenging oil burner the oil may form coke in the burner tip due to heat from gas
burner.

During the scavenging process, remaining fuel oil traces inside the burner is steam
flushed. The heavy oil (fuel traces) need to be burnt while scavenging or otherwise
it will be deposited on the ignitors/adjacent burners/ tubes. To burn this Fuel Oil
traces, pilot flame or gas flame need to be established during scavenging process.
19.7 WHRS Normal Shutdown Procedure:
1. Inform to concerned units FCCU-II & YSF about the shutdown activity and the
duration.
2. Ensure alternative arrangements are lined up, flue gas to be diverted to boiler stack.
3. Slowly isolate the damper towards WHRS and ensure no passing of flue gas.
4. Maintain the steam drum level till the temperature comes down.
5. Inform to FCCU-II / FGD-II to stop water circulation pump.
19.8 WHRS Dry Run Procedure (To be used during Emergency Operation)
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:19 Rev. No.2 Page 174 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 19.8 WHRS Dry Run Procedure (To be used during Emergency Operation)

content:
A. The Following are the standard operating conditions of WHRS
1. Evaporation capacity: 6.1 TPH
2. Maximum working pressure: 12.5 Kg/cm2 (g)
3. Feed water Temperature: 115 Deg. C
4. Steam Drum Outlet Pressure: 10.5 Kg/cm2 (g)
5. Steam Temp. At steam drum outlet: 185 Deg. C
6. Flue gas inlet Temperature (Operating): 320 Deg.C
7. Flue Gas flow (Operating): 87779 Kg/Hr.
8. Flue Gas Outlet Temperature: 200 Deg. C
B. General in all condition:
The practice of operating a waste heat recovery boiler without circulation through the tubes
should be avoided except in emergency situations. The following normal dry running
procedures must be adhered to:
 Inform respective Management Office regarding the situation.
 Read instruction manual section before operating the equipment in a dry condition.
 The flue gas outlet temperature must be no higher than the 250/260 Deg. C. Any
temperature above 300 Deg. C can be disturbing for boiler, dry run operation in
emergency condition also.
 The period of dry running must be as short as possible.
 Our recommendation is 30 – 40 minutes.
 Prior to dry running the tube banks must be cleaned to remove all traces of soot and
other accumulations.
 Cleaning by soot blowing must continue throughout the dry running period with at least
the same frequency as for normal operation.
 All internal must be inspected before and after dry run operation for any type of deposit,
Sulphur retention of any soot and any loosening abnormality.
 The tube bank must be drained and vented.
 Insure exhaust temperature should always be lower than inlet temperature.
 As good engineering practice, boiler dry run operation is to be undertaken during
emergency situation and temperature must be monitored closely to be below 300 Deg.
C maximum. In case dry run operation is required frequently this is not good operation
practice, should be avoided.
C) Dry Run without water Inside of the pressure parts:
For dry run all water inside the pressure parts should be removed. All vent (steam drum,
Evaporator header, Economizer header and start up vent) valve should be open. By
operating IBD valve we can remove all boiler water from the steam drum. To remove water
from economizer and evaporator coils, open the drain valve of the bottom header of these
coils. Also open the drain valve of down comer so that all boiler water from the pressure
part will be removed.
During dry run flue gas inlet temperature should lesser than operating temperature (300
Deg.C). Soot blower should be operating frequently. Flue gas outlet temperature should not
more than the inlet temperature. Monitoring inlet and outlet flue gas temperature is
important to ensure no sudden rise occurs.
Temperature excursion may indicate the presence of a soot fire. Dry run period should
not more than 30 minutes. Dry run should not be frequently practices otherwise tube and
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:19 Rev. No.2 Page 175 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > C. Dry Run without water Inside of the pressure parts:

content:
other parts life will be drastically reduced. All internal must be inspected before and after
dry run operation for any type of deposit, Sulphur retention of any soot and any loosening
abnormality.
D) Dry Run procedure water inside (Black out) in emergency case:
This condition will be arrived because of suddenly feed water flow cut off, feed water pump
breakdown; feed water pump motor was not working, power supply of motor fails. At this
black out condition, all drains and vents of the boiler should be closed. Monitor temperature
and pressure of the boiler. When pressure of the boiler reaches to the maximum allowable
pressure, crack open the startup vent valve and maintain boiler pressure just below the
maximum allowable pressure. Maintain this situation still another feed water pump takes
in line or power supply resume. In this condition to maintain steam drum level in between
normal to low level.
Total volume of pressure part is around 12 Mtr.3. Design flow of boiler is 6.1 TPH and
operating flow is 5.1 TPH. In this situation boiler can be run maximum 60 / 70 minutes. If
dry run increased more than this, it will be cause of premature failure of the tubes. During
black out, soot blower should be operated frequently to avoid soot deposition on the coils.
RAV should be operated continuously.
All internal must be inspected before and after dry run operation for any type of deposit,
Sulphur retention of any soot and any loosening abnormality.
