breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > CHAPTER - 20

content:
20.1. Temporary Operations:
To improve the Equipment / plant performance, certain temporary operations are to be
carried out periodically. Those are listed below.
20.1.1. Preservation FCCU-I & FCCU-II CO Boilers:
Boiler though healthy, deteriorates due to corrosion if precautions not taken for
preservation when it is idle for a long time.
The principal cause for corrosion is the oxygen ingress in to the Boiler when not in
service. The oxygen corrosion can usually be recognized from pitting, thinning out etc.
The corrosion also can take place outside of drum and tube due to presence of moisture
in air.
The oxygen corrosion results in thinning out of materials isolated pit or crater,
introduction of loose oxides in the piping system and failures resulting from these three
factors.
The boiler requires preservation inside as well outside right from the period of hydraulic
test when water is first introduced into the boiler till boiler comes on line or boiler kept
idle for long period after use.
The following Procedure to be followed for fire side preservation:
a. Keep the Silica Gel (200Kgs) for each Boiler ready in powder / bead form.
b. Operate all soot blowers several times before shutting down the Boiler, to remove as
much deposit and soot as possible. Start the last operating sequence shall start with
the soot blower at the fire box side and follow the flue gas path to the blower nearest
the stack.
c. Open the furnace manhole.
d. Clean the furnace floor.
e. Blank off all (top and bottom) burners openings from the fire box side before water
washing to avoid wetting metal parts of the burner.
f. A cover of plastic or other available material should be used to protect the refractory
from wash water.
g. Firing oil with ash content leads to deposition of some ash on the boiler and super
heater surfaces. It is composed of insoluble residue along with a bonding material,
which adheres to the boiler and super heater surfaces. This densely bonded mass
may not be completely removed by normal cleaning devices as soot blowers.
The boiler should be cooled to prevent spalling and stresses in the refractory from
the wash water. If the unit is cool enough for workmen to stay inside the fire box
then the refractory is cool enough to permit washing. It is preferable that the
refractory retains some heat to aid in drying (from inside) at the completion of wash.
After the Boiler (fire side) is cooled to 45oC, water wash the Boiler, economiser on
the flue gas side (as shown in attached drawing). Carry out the final washing from
top to bottom completing it while the Boiler is still warm.
h. When washing, efforts should be made to direct the water on the heaviest
accumulations. A large quantity of water is to be used so as to flush out the insoluble
material and to dilute the acid forming elements in the ash. Manual cleaning to be
done to dislodge the more tenacious accumulation on the super heater elements.
Care should be taken to avoid damage to the tubes when using rods or bar during
this exercise.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:20 Rev. No.2 Page 179 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > The following Procedure to be followed for fire side preservation

content:
i. It is extremely important, once washing is started, to clean the surface down to bare
metal. Otherwise corrosion is encouraged; the deposits which remain accelerate on a
round-the-clock basis until the boiler surfaces are clean.
j. As soon as the boiler is washed, it should be dried out with compressed air
immediately.
k. Before start up, dry out of heater to be carried out.
l. Place silica gel (One drum i.e., 200 kg) in the fire box. Lime trays require raking
once a month and replacement after six months. Silica is placed when it changes
colour.
m. Close all openings, Sealing as necessary.
n. Close the stack damper completely.
The following Procedure to be followed for tube side preservation:
a. Refer to the following P&IDs for Nitrogen hose connection at Economiser inlet,
super heater outlet.
FCCU-I CO Boiler: VR-04-COB-00106 (Attached)
FCCU-II CO Boiler: VR-14-F-03-0051(Attached)
Nitrogen headers to be charged.
b. Positively isolate the Boiler by installing blinds.
c. Keep the following drain valves open:
i. Economiser bottom Header
ii. Steam drum
iii. Water drum
iv. Super heater inlet
v. Super heater outlet
d. Keep the vent closed.
e. Blow the Boiler with Nitrogen in forward direction:
For FCCU-I CO Boiler, connect Nitrogen hose to vent valve (V1) at economizer
inlet and purge Nitrogen through drain valve (V2) of pressure gauge at super heater
outlet as shown in P&ID.
For FCCU-II CO Boiler, connect Nitrogen hose to vent valve (V1) at economizer
inlet and purge Nitrogen through drain valve (V2) of pressure gauge at super heater
outlet as shown in P&ID.
Open Nitrogen inlet valve at economizer (V1&V3) as indicated in P&ID and purge
it at the outlet of super heater (V2&V4) as indicated in P&ID, continue Nitrogen
blowing in forward direction for 4 hours
f. Blow the Boiler with Nitrogen in reverse direction.
After 4 hours, close V1 &V3 and disconnect Nitrogen hoses at V1 & V3. Connect
Nitrogen hoses to spools at the outlet of super heater (V2&V4 respectively as
indicated in P&ID). Open valve at economizer inlet (V1&V3 respectively as
indicated in P&ID).
Open Nitrogen inlet valve at outlet of super heater (V2) and purge it through V1 at
economizer inlet. Continue Nitrogen blowing from reverse direction for 4 hours.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:20 Rev. No.2 Page 180 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > The following Procedure to be followed for tube side preservation

content:
g. Continue the above procedure till moisture in the loop is removed (no visible white
fog at vent outlet).
h. Isolate Nitrogen valves.
i. Close all the five drain valves as mentioned above.
j. Crack open the vent valves (V2&V4).
k. Start Nitrogen purging through V1&V3.
l. Check the oxygen content in the Nitrogen in the steam circuit. If the Oxygen
present is less than 0.4%, then close the vent valves.
m. Monitor by keeping regular watch on pressure of BFW inlet, economizer inlet and
steam drum and shiftily reading should be logged. It should be around 4 to
6kg/cm2. Pressure up with Nitrogen through V1& V3, if the pressure comes down.
n. Field observations to be logged during preservation, i.e, Pressure of the steam drum
and color of Silica gel.
o. Chemicals are to be handled as per the safety procedure given in MSDS
(Attached).
p. Oxygen checking to be done before giving any entry permit.
q. Responsibility of the operations is to implement the procedure and continuous
monitoring of steam pressure.
r. Periodic monitoring of silica gel color and replacement of thereof by
operations/Technical.
WHRS Preservation:
WHRS Boiler though healthy, deteriorates due to corrosion if precautions not taken for
preservation when it is idle for a long time.
The principal cause for corrosion is the oxygen ingress in to the Boiler when not in
service. The oxygen corrosion can usually be recognized from pitting, thinning out etc.
The corrosion also can take place outside of drum and tube due to presence of moisture
in air.
The oxygen corrosion results in thinning out of materials isolated pit or crater,
introduction of loose oxides in the piping system and failures resulting from these three
factors.
The boiler requires preservation inside as well outside right from the period of hydraulic
test when water is first introduced into the boiler till boiler comes on line or boiler kept
idle for long period after use.
20.1.2. Procedure for Boiler Burner Scanners Bypass
Sometimes Boiler burner scanners are to be bypassed for various reasons like poor
sensing, malfunctioning, under maintenance and non-availability of spares. During the
bypass time of scanners, the frequency of manual observation of fuel firing in boiler
furnace is to be increased than the normal observation.
Boiler burner scanner is a safety device used to protect the boiler, operating
personnel and the environment in case of flame failure. Fuel supply into the hot
furnace in absence of flame will lead to furnace explosion. Scanner working
condition is an important factor to be monitored all the time.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:20 Rev. No.2 Page 181 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.2 Procedure for Boiler Burner Scanners Bypass

content:
For sensing the flame in the furnace, boilers are equipped with flame scanners. Each
fuel (fuel oil & fuel gas) burner is provided with a scanner called “Main flame
scanner”. To ignite the main fuel, an ignitor is required, called pilot burner and is
provided with a scanner called “Pilot flame scanner”. These scanners are to be in
service to safeguard the boiler operation.
The scanners setup of the FCCU-I & II CO Boilers is as follows:
Boiler is equipped with two main flame scanners for top burner (one for oil& one for
gas) and two for bottom burner (one for oil & one for gas). Two pilot flame
scanners, also provided, one for top burner and one for bottom burner.
The various alternate protection plans during the bypass of scanners are listed below.
1. Viewing through peep hole very often. (max interval 30 min)
2. Viewing the stack smoke very often. (max interval 30 min)
3. Watching the fuel oil/ fuel gas burner and header pressures on the DCS screen by
the DCS Incharge. (max interval 30 min)
4. Boiler steam header steady flow and pressure on the DCS screen are the direct
indications that flame is there in the boiler. (max interval 30 min)
5. Periodical cleaning of peep hole is to be ensured to maintain the good visibility
of the flame pattern. (once in a week)
20.1.3. Refinery Fuel Gas System Management and Control as applicable to Power
Plants
The following instruction needs to be followed as a Fuel Gas Consuming Units to
contribute for NIL Flaring of Fuel Gas for any reason as it is a direct loss of Fuel and
on the other hand; LPG vaporization, if any (for Fuel Gas make up) leads to ‘Fuel
quality giveaway’ which tantamount to burning of LPG instead of LSHS . This gives
way for the Fuel Gas Controlling unit (normally FCCU2) to better balance the
refinery Fuel Gas generation and consumption.
1. During startup of the units, consume minimum quantity of Fuel Gas. Inform to Fuel
Gas controlling unit in advance.
2. During normal run, maintain constant consumption of Fuel Gas. In case of any
change in Fuel Gas consumption, prior permission from the F.G controlling unit
need to be obtained in co-ordination with YSF.
3. Follow the instructions from Fuel Gas controlling unit for increase / decrease in
Fuel Gas consumption as dual firing facility is available. Generally FCCU – II is
the controlling unit of the Fuel Gas system, unless otherwise instructed through
DOB.
4. Check the Fuel Gas Knock out Drum level frequently and drain the liquid carryover
if any. In case of any upsets in SRU (if informed by SRU personnel), check it
frequently every 15 minutes. Continuously monitor if the liquid carry over is
continuous.
5. Make changes in the Fuel Gas consumption when Yard Shift Foreman advices upon
requirement of the Fuel Gas Controlling Unit to match the total generation &
consumption.
20.1.4. Procedure for Draining liquid from Fuel Gas knockout drum
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:20 Rev. No.2 Page 182 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.4 Procedure for Draining liquid from Fuel Gas knockout drum

content:
The following Procedure to be followed while draining liquid from Fuel Gas
knockout drum for safely routing liquid to OWS and avoid fuel gas routing to
OWS.
1. There is one fuel gas knockout drum at PP-2 to separate liquid from gas incase liquid
carryover takes place along with fuel gas due to process upset.
2. The original provision was to route the liquid to FCCU-I fuel gas absorber drum.
3. Consequent to FCCU-I become obsolete, the liquid was being drained to empty
containers by connecting a hose to the vessel drain and subsequently the liquid was
being transferred to OWS.
4. Since the above practice was not safe, as an alternate, it was advised by technical to
route the liquid to nearby OWS through pipe line. The new underground line was
connected to OWS near North-East corner of FCCU-II CO Boiler.
5. Fuel gas routing to OWS can cause escaping of gaseous HC to atmosphere, which is
unsafe practice. Hence the procedure to be followed to prevent escaping of gas to
OWS.
6. The liquid carryover in Fuel gas knock out drum to be observed once in two hours
in normal time and more frequently during process units offset and startup.
7. If the liquid level is observed in gauge glass, the liquid to be drained to OWS through
pipe line. While draining following procedure to be followed.
8. Ensure no hot work near the fuel gas knockout drum and near OWS pit north-east
corner of FCCU-II CO Boiler. Drain till the liquid is visible in gauge glass. The
moment liquid level goes below the visible level in glass close the 1st isolation drain
valve. This is done to maintain a liquid level in the bottom and prevent escaping of
gas to OWS.
9. After draining the liquid, flush the drain line with service water by connecting hose
to the flushing point in between 1st isolation valve and 2nd Isolation valve on drain
line. After flushing close the flushing point and disconnect the hose and cap the
point.
10. Close the 2nd isolation drain valve after flushing.
11. The area near knockout drum and near OWS to be checked with HC detector for any
presence of hydrocarbons.
20.1.5. Procedures for Releasing the Pressure Safety Valves where ever more than one
PSVs are available for Periodical Inspection & Maintenance.
The following procedure to be adopted while releasing PSVs for Periodical inspection
and maintenance
1. For online releasing of those PSVs to periodical inspection, which remains in service
as 100 % standby on the equipment and having isolation valves; subject to the
Technical and Inspection clearance to those valves which can be released for annual
inspection and periodical Maintenance inorder to ensure the satisfactory
performance while in service.
2. The following equipment in Utilities is having more than one Pressure Safety Valve.
a) VHP-MP R/S C/V (VREP-2) of PP-II – 2
b) MP-LP R/S C/V (VREP-2) of PP-II – 3
3. This Plant Standing Instruction is aimed at evolving a measure to ensure the
healthiness of the PSVs on the Steam Lines and Equipment having continuous
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:20 Rev. No.2 Page 183 of 251

content:
service, which PSVs are provided as 100% standby with isolation valve for releasing
to Valve Section as per Technical and Inspection advice.
4. All the PSVs should be kept in service on the available equipment, with the isolation
valves in open position where ever isolation valve is available.
5. These PSVs covered in these guidelines does not deal with the IBR Boilers; as the
Boiler PSVs are overhauled and inspected during IBR inspection and also live test is
ensured for proper functioning of the same, while the Boiler is re-started after IBR
inspection.

7. Subject to the Inspection and Technical concurrence, the above PSVs to be released
for Annual inspection and periodical Maintenance by raising the Work Requisition
to the Valve Section and also Work Requisition for Safety Valve (WRSV) for
maintenance needs that are to be carried out on the PSV having isolation valve and
is 100% Standby needed to be released as per Inspection and Technical Plan.
20.1.6. Procedure For Boiler Fuel Oil Burners Gun Cleaning
1. This procedure is developed for the following purposes
a) To avoid incomplete combustion inside furnace due to oil dipping/improper fuel
oil atomization because of oil burner plugging and to overcome boiler
shutdown/plant upset and hazards.
b) To eliminate the chances of potential nearmiss due to accumulation of
combustibles inside furnace causing fire explosion.
c) To ensure environmental friendly operation by reducing emissions through stack
due to incomplete combustion. To improve boiler reliability & performance by
ensuring proper combustion & heat distribution.
2. The following Boilers are having fuel oil/ fuel gas provisions like:
a) FCCU-I(R) New CO Boiler: Four Nos oil/gas firing burners with process
exhaust CO gas intake from FCCU-I(R) unit.
b) FCCU-II Old CO Boiler: Two Nos oil/gas firing burners with process exhaust
CO gas intake from FCCU-II unit, but at present Oil firing is not in use.
3. In case of fuel oil firing there is chance of furnace inside oil dripping/coke formation,
burner plugging/clogging due to some probable cause of improper atomization/ lack
of heat tracing/ presence of foreign particles in fuel oil/ improper air distribution/
insufficient air for combustion etc.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:20 Rev. No.2 Page 184 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.6 Procedure For Boiler Fuel Oil Burners Gun Cleaning

content:
4. As a result which could lead to fire explosion/hazards due to oil accumulation,
unplanned boiler shutdown/plant upset due to tube/refractory failure because of
uneven firing pattern /flame impingement on furnace wall.
5. Periodical oil gun cleaning is planned as unit level maintenance activity from safety
and reliability point of view.
6. Clean each fuel oil burner once in month on DRJ basis or clean it when there is such
following observations:

Individual burner fuel oil pressure is holding on higher side even at low fuel oil
flow

Fuel oil dripping from burner
 Coke formation on fuel oil burners
 Flame pattern is not good ie. flickering, short, uneven, impingement even after
all adjustments.

Fuel oil/steam leak from burner assembly

Boiler taking for shut down etc.
7. Take DCS shift in charge concurrence before initiation of oil gun dropping activity
and & placing back the oil fire in the respective burner so that he can co-ordinate &
adjust the loads accordingly.
8. Ensure that gas fire and pilot are there in the particular burner if it’s in dual firing
mode or ensure the healthiness of firing and pilot in the other burner if it’s only in
single oil firing mode.
9. Remove the oil fire by stopping from BMS panel & close fuel oil block valve, then
adjust the firing accordingly in the other burners.
10. Do scavenging the oil gun at least thrice by atomizing steam until the flame
disappeared & ensure only dry steam is getting purged into oil gun. Then close
atomizing steam block valve after scavenging.
11. Don’t keep oil burner more time inside without steam purging esp. when there is gas
firing which will lead to burner tip detoriation due to high heat radiation.
12. Close the individual air resister to the burner if there is no gas fire in the same burner
as it will lead to incomplete combustion in the other burner and will cause smoke
even after maintaining excess air to furnace & higher O2% at stack outlet.
13. Disconnect the oil hose and atomizing steam hose from oil gun after ensuring no
passing from oil & atomizing steam block valves.
14. Remove the oil gun from the burner assembly by using proper PPE ie. Wearing face
shield & asbestos gloves.
15. Keep the oil gun outside & purge it again with necessary steam hose arrangement
until it’s cleared of all oil traces & then let it gets completely cooled to work on it.
16. Fix the oil gun properly in the bench vice, then open & clean all the gun parts
thoroughly with kerosene/ atomizing steam/ plant air. If any parts found damaged
then replace with the spare ones.
17. Take utmost care while reassembling the oil gun as wrong threading or over
tightening may damage the oil gun parts.
18. Check for any leaks with help of steam, then fix the oil gun in the burner assembly
& connect oil/ atomizing steam hoses with copper gaskets/ ‘O’ rings.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:20 Rev. No.2 Page 185 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.6 Procedure For Boiler Fuel Oil Burners Gun Cleaning

content:
19. Purge/ scavenge the burner with atomizing steam for clearing out any hydrocarbon
traces & again for checking any leaks.
20. Inform DCS shift in charge for lightening up the oil burner as per BMS logic after
normalizing air resister, fuel oil & atomizing steam.
21. Adjust fuel oil & atomizing steam pressure at burner as per burner design datasheet
or 1 kg/cm2 to 2 kg/cm2 on thumb rule. Do fine adjustments of air resisters/ burner
alignment to avoid flame impingement & to ensure bright uniform flame.
22. Update the burner spare resister & if required raise MIT to maintain the desired
burner spares inventory.
20.1.7. Trips/Interlocks Management Procedure
1. All trips and interlocks should always be online.
2. If any particular trip/ interlock is to be bypassed/ forced for any maintenance work,
it should be with the concurrence of unit-in-charge (during day shift of working day)
or Division Head and the required entries in the trip register should be made. In off
day shift hours, YSF/Unit-in-charge/ Division Head of Operations are authorised to
grant permission.
3. Any trip/ interlock not on line for more than 72 hrs. has to be intimated to DGM-
Operations, DGM-Maintenance in the specified format (Annexure-I).
4. Any trip/ interlock not on line for more than 7days, has to be intimated to GM-
Operations in the specified Format (Annexure-II).
5. For all trips/ logistics, Instrument maintenance (in consultation with operation)
should develop and implement suitable preventive maintenance checks at suitable
periodicity.

22.2 Procedure for sample collection:
Procedure for collecting samples at various locations and the safety involved in
collecting the samples is described below.
(1) Use only plastic bottles while collecting samples for MSL to avoid silica leaching
in glass bottle. Remaining water samples can be collected in Glass bottles.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:22 Rev. No.2 Page 193 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 13 14-PSV-7505 > 22.2 Procedure for sample collection:

content:
(2) Sample should be collected after 5 min of flushing to avoid contamination from
stagnated water in the sample line. Keep the valve opening 1-2 threads to maintain
smooth flow through sample line in such a way that it should not form aeration in
sample bottle to avoid error in sampling and absorption / dissolution of present gases
in collected sample?
(3) Wash the sample bottles thrice and collect the sample up to full level in sample
bottle.
(4) Testing of collected samples is to be carried out at the earliest in the laboratory.
(5) In case of abnormal result, check sample should be sent at the earliest to confirm
abnormal parameters and to avoid its effect on downstream quality.
(6) Place the sample bottle at the designated location (samples work bench). Insist
sample boy to keep the empty bottles on the same bench aside.
Safety involved in sample collection:
CBD & MSL sample is very hot, they need to cool completely in cooler then only
samples to be cooled.

23.3. Max Storage Allowable In The Plant:
An inventory corresponding to around 30 days chemical consumption (buffer stock) is

23.4. Procedure for Process Chemical Usage in PP-2:
Chemical treatment in Boilers is used to maintain Steam quality and to protect steam
generation equipment from corrosion and deposits.
Chemical treatment in bearing cooling water (BCW) system is done to prevent bio-
organism growth , scale formation and corrosion.

24.0. Intoduction:
It is necessary that each plant personnel is familiar with the nature of the material in the
process area, aware of the hazard to his own and to other people's health, the fire and
explosion danger, and should know the protective measures to be taken
24.1. Chemicals Hazards in the plant:
The following are the Hazards associated with the chemicals are due to chemical ingestion
or contamination:
Skin : Irritation with corrosive destruction at site of contact. Pain can be delayed up to
three or more minutes after exposure, but tissue destruction begins immediately on
contact.
Eye: Severe tissue destruction, corneal pacification, and possible loss of vision.
Lung: Causes small burns in the respiratory tract with corrosive destruction to the
mucosa, loss of hair in the nasal passages, and possible delayed pulmonary oedema (fluid
in the lungs), which can be fatal.
Ingestion: results in serious, life-threatening destruction of tissues along the digestive
tract. Death can occur within days as a result of shock, asphyxia (due to blocked airways
caused by the swelling of the oesophagus and associated tissues), or pneumonia.
24.2. Special or Unique Hazards:
24.2.1. Chemical spillage:
As the chemical to be handled frequently in the plant, There is possibility of chemical
spillage on the operating personnel. Standard procedures developed for chemical handling
to be followed for chemical handling mentioned in chapter-23.
24.3. First Aid Facilities:
24.3.1. First aid procedures:
The following are the first aid measures in various cases of hazards
Sl Hazard/ Injury First Aid Procedure
1. Chemical Eye Check for and remove any contact lenses. In case of contact,
Contact immediately flush eyes with plenty of water for at least 15
minutes. Cold water may be used. Get medical attention
immediately
2. Chemical Skin In case of contact, immediately flush skin with plenty of water
Contact for at least 15 minutes while removing contaminated clothing
and shoes. Cover the irritated skin with an emollient. Cold
water may be used. Wash clothing before reuse. Thoroughly
clean shoes before reuse. Get medical attention immediately
In case of Serious Skin Contact:
Wash with a disinfectant soap and cover the contaminated skin
with an anti-bacterial cream. Seek immediate medical
attention.
3. Chemical If inhaled, remove to fresh air. If not breathing, give artificial
Inhalation respiration. If breathing is difficult, give oxygen. Get medical
attention immediately.
In case of Chemical Serious Inhalation:
Evacuate the victim to a safe area as soon as possible. Loosen
tight clothing such as a collar, tie, belt or waistband. If
breathing is difficult, administer oxygen. If the victim is not

content:
Sl Hazard/ Injury First Aid Procedure
breathing, perform mouth-to-mouth resuscitation.
WARNING: It may be hazardous to the person providing aid
to give mouth-to-mouth resuscitation when the inhaled
material is toxic, infectious or corrosive. Seek immediate
medical attention
4. Chemical If swallowed, do not induce vomiting unless directed to do so
Ingestion by medical personnel. Never give anything by mouth to an
uFCCU-1 COnscious person. Loosen tight clothing such as a
collar, tie, belt or waistband. Get medical attention
immediately.
5. Chemical In case of burns, the following first aid has to be provided:
Burns:  Remove patient from accident site

Wash injury with tepid water liberally

Identify chemical for effective therapy

Seek medical treatment

IV fluids need to be administered

Pain medications and antibiotics needed

Wounds cleaned and bandaged

Follow -up care compulsory

Consultation with specialist is a must
6. Chemical In case of Chemical splash in the eye, the following first aid
Splash in the has to be provided:
Eye  Lay the person on the floor

Keep eye lids open forcibly

Use clean cold water to wash eyes gently

Keep washing steadily for at least 20 minutes

Rinse/wash hands thoroughly to remove chemical

If wearing contact lens remove them

Do not rub eyes

Do not use eye drops until told

Wear sunglasses to minimize irritation

After these basic steps seek medical help

Remember the name of chemical to tell doctor
7. Dislocation: In case of dislocation, the following first aid has to be
provided:
 Call medical help as soon as possible
 Do not move the joint or try to place it back
 Place ice to control swelling
 If skin is cut, clean gently and bandage with sterile
gauze

Sling or splint the injury in its original position

If injury is serious, check for breathing

If not breathing, provide Cardio pulmonary resuscitation
(CPR). Elevate the feet up to 12 inches

Cover the patient with a blanket.
8. Electric Shock In case of Electric shock, the following first aid has to be
provided:

Do not attempt to move the victim from current source

Sl Hazard/ Injury First Aid Procedure

First step is to switch off the current source

Otherwise, move the source using a wooden stick

Attend to the victim

Check for breathing

No breathing, do Cardio pulmonary resuscitation (CPR)

Call emergency medical aid

If breathing, do a physical examination

Treat for minor burns

Re-establish vital functions

Excessive burns may require hospitalization/ surgery

Supportive care must be provided
9. Fracture There are two types of fractures.

Open fracture: Skin breaks causing open wound

Closed fracture: Skin not broken
In case of both the fractures, the first aid differs and is
followed as discussed below:
10. Sprain In case of sprain, the following first aid has to be provided:

Apply a Compress to injured area for 20 min

This may be done 4-8 times a day

Use a plastic bag with crushed ice, wrapped in a towel

Use compression bandages to reduce swelling

Keep the injured leg elevated on a pillow

Take anti-inflammatory pills if necessary

Take rest for the recommended period

When pain/swelling is diminished, do recommended
exercises
11. Heart Attack In case of a heart attack, the following first aid has to be
provided:

Try to relax

Loosen tight clothes

Take medicines if any

Pain subsides within 3 min of medicine intake

If not, see a doctor.

Give artificial respiration if required

Give Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR):
a) If no pulse is detected
b) By placing palm on chest to pump

15 pumps are followed by 2 artificial respiration
Continue till ambulance / doctor arrives
12. Heat Stroke In case of a heat stroke, the following first aid has to be
provided:

Remove the person to a shady place

Cool the person by sponging with wet towel

Apply ice packs in armpits and groin

Water with electrolyte, fruit / vegetable juice should be
given

Victim must be rested.

content:
Sl Hazard/ Injury First Aid Procedure
13. Severe Bleeding In case of severe bleeding, the following first aid has to be
provided:

Wash hands well before administering to patient

Wear synthetic gloves

Make the victim lie down

Slightly elevate the legs

If possible keep the affected area elevated
 Remove any obvious debris/particle

Apply direct pressure using clean cloth/bandage

Use hand if cloth is not available

Apply pressure continuously for at least 20 minutes

Do not remove the cloth to check the bleeding

Hold the bandage in place using an adhesive tape
 If bleeding seeps through bandage, do not remove it

Add extra bandage on top of the first one

Apply direct pressure on the artery if necessary

The pressure points for arm--below arm- pit/above
elbow

For leg--behind knee/near groin

Squeeze the artery keeping finger flat

Continue applying pressure on the wound

Once bleeding stops immobilize the affected part

See a doctor
14. Spinal Cord In case of spinal cord injury, seek medical help or call for an
Injury ambulance as soon as possible. Meanwhile the following
first aid has to be provided -

Move the person, if surrounding is not safe

Immobilize the head, neck and body on both sides

Movements may dislocate vertebra and cause further
injury

If there is no sign of breathing, perform CPR

Do not tilt head backward during CPR.

content:
The First Aid Box provided in the control rooms should be in sealed condition by cello
tape / brown tape (not lock or chain) normally. The purpose of this sealing is not to prevent
usage of the contents. It is for the purpose to ensure that if any person uses the same it is
recorded in TOB with reasons so that the root cause of the injury is eliminated.
In every shift, the field TOB should be recorded that “the contents of the First Aid Box are
intact”. If any item is used from the First Aid Box in the shift, it should to be recorded in
shift log, with proper reasons.
Refinery First Aid Boxes check-up and replacement of First Aid Material is done by the
Dispensary staff in every 3rd week of the month. In case the First Aid Box is used during
the month before the regular checking, the supervisor in charge of the First Aid Box (shift)
is advised to inform the Dispensary immediately to get replenishment of the First Aid
Material with a memo signed by the plant Manager. First Aid material is available with
Dispensary doctor and pharmacist in all the shifts for all emergency cases.
List of First Aid personnel available in the field
24.4. Personnel protective Equipment availability and usage in the Plant:
24.4.1.Commonly used PPE and its Functions:
24.4.1.1. Canisters:
Before using canister select right canister for giving protection against the gas
encountered. This can be done by checking the color code.
Color code for canisters generally used in the refinery.
Canister Color of Canister
Acid Gas White
Organic vapor Black
Ammonia Gas Green
TEL Black tin
Make sure that the life of the chemicals inside the canister is not expired. The expiry of
the canister can be ascertained by three ways.
The life of canister is 5 years if it is not exposed to atmosphere (if seals are not open).
Generally expiry date is marked on canister.
The life of the canister is one year if the seals are opened and the canister is exposed to
atmosphere.
The life of the canister is 100 minutes if it is used in contaminated atmosphere.
For a human being to survive at least 16% of Oxygen in air by volume (for a safe side
17%) is essential. As the canister is only air purifying devices, before using the same,
make sure that the percentage of oxygen is minimum 17% by volume in air in the
place where it is to be used.
The canister can give protection up to 2% (By volume) of the toxic gases in
atmosphere. If the percentage of toxic gas is more than 2% by volume in the
atmosphere, the canister cannot give 100% protection. So make sure the toxic gas
percentage is below 2% in volume.
The canister can give protection against only one type of gas. If the atmosphere has
got different gases (exam: Flare gas contains organic vapor, H2S, NOX etc.,) the
canister cannot give the required protection. So identify nature of the contaminator.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:24 Rev. No.2 Page 211 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 24.0 Intoduction: > 24.4 Personnel protective Equipment availability and usage in the Plant: > 24.4.1.1 Canisters:

content:
Both the seals (top to bottom) of canister to be opened before use. Then connect the
threaded end of the canister to the face mask and tie the harness of the canister to the
body of the user. Now the equipment is ready for use.
 In case of skin irritation, body should be covered with personal protecting
clothing.
 After usage date and period of exposure to be recorded on the canister.
24.4.1.2.Face mask:
Put on the face mask and check it for air tightness. This can be done by closing the
end of the breathing tube by hand while face mask is in use and if the user is feeling
suffocated then it is considered leak proof. If not, further tighten the harness or
replace the mask. If the breathing tube is having any kink (bend), it should not be
used.
24.4.1.3.Chemical cartridge Respirator:
It consists of a half mask provided with one or two cartridge. Like canisters these
cartridge respirators are filled with certain chemicals for protection against specific
contaminants. These are having very limited use like canister mask. These are used
only in open atmosphere.
24.4.1.4.Dust Respirator:
Dust respirators are used in the dusty atmosphere. The simplest type is surgical cotton
mask. In case cartridge type Dust mask, the face piece should be adjusted so as to fit
snugly, on the face.
Limitation:
i. It should not be used in closed vessels, where there is a chance of Oxygen
deficiency.
ii. It should not be used if it has been used somebody else and not sterilized.
24.4.1.5.Panorama goggles:
These goggles provided protection against acid/alkali splashes, chemicals and oils
etc., they are required to be worn in areas where possibility of a splash exists.
24.4.1.6.Head protection:
Safety helmets are designed to protect the head, not only from impact but from flying
particles and electric shock or combination of the three.
24.4.1.7.Hand protection:
Protective gloves are required to be used for protection of the hands against the effect
of chemicals, heat heavy material, oil, electricity etc., PVC gloves give protection
against chemicals and solvent. Chemicals or oil should be washed from the gloves
after use. Canvas gloves should be used while handling material or working an
equipment where there is possibility of getting cuts and abrasion on hand. Electrical
gloves should be used by employees while working around high voltage lines. These
should thoroughly inspected before use. Asbestos gloves should be used for protecting
hands against heat.
24.4.1.8.Foot protection:
Rubber gum boots are used to protect against chemical splashes; safety shoes are used
to protect against heavy weight. Rubber gum boots are recommended during work in
water/mud.

content:
24.4.1.9.Body protection:
1. PVC Aprons provide protection for the body when working with chemicals,
aromatics or solvents.
2. PVC Dress (Hood, Coat and Pant) gives complete protection against contact of
acids/alkalies and chemicals with the body part.
3. Ear Plugs is inserted into the ear canals and varies considerably both in design and
material. Materials used are rubber, soft hard plastic, foam or cotton.
4. Ear Muffs cover the external ear to provide an acoustic barrier.
5. Safety belt (IS-3521) with a lifeline fastened at the back should always be used
whenever one works on an elevated location or enters into tanks or vessels so that
he can be easily lifted and brought out. Belt and harness should be nylon chrome-
tanned leather or cotton webbing.
Life belt must be inspected careful, before each use. If any sign of damaged found
to be discarded immediately. The lifeline must be carefully inspected for any sign
of damage on wear, it in doubt replace the rope. Be sure that the lifeline is securely
attached to the string of the belt.
While in use anchor the belt to a fixed point on structure and secure it properly. The
‘D’ shackle provided with belt must be used for anchoring.
Keep the belt clean, dry and in good condition. Your life is depending on it.

Compressors and pumps etc.
 Wear ear plugs during soot blowing, attending heavy steam leaks etc.

Noise exposure time limits to be followed ( at a distance of 1m from source)

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 24.0 Intoduction: > 25.4 -- Contaminated Rain water system (CRWS)

content:
25.0. Plant Drainage System Description:
Generally the Plant Drainage system consists of Closed Blow down (CBD) System, Oily
Water Sewer (OWS), Surface Drain System (SS) and Contaminated Rain water system
(CRWS). Each system is described below.
25.1. Closed Blow Down (CBD) System:
CBD network helps in reducing amount of hydrocarbon loss. Contaminated
hydrocarbons from equipment draining is collected and routed to CBD vessel via CBD
headers running across the entire unit.
No such CBD network is existing in Power Plant-II.
25.2. Oily Water Sewer (OWS):
This system also called OWS is mainly to collect water contaminated with hydrocarbon
oil. Such streams are generated during equipment draining and flushing during routine
operation. Streams suitable for OWS also generated as a result of floor washing and
cleaning or spilled oil etc. Drained streams received through OWS funnels etc. are
routed to ETP-I through a combined header.
The OWS manholes are sealed and a vent pipe provided releases any hydrocarbon
trapped at a higher elevation. There is one OWS manhole point provided at FCCU-2
CO Boiler in Power Plant-II. All manhole vents are of 4” diameter. OWS funnels at
equipment drain points are of 6”diameter. All U/G piping is coated with an external
tape.
The effluent generated in the unit reaches ETP-I for treatment and oil recovery. Care
should be taken that at no time any spark reaches vapor space of OWS funnels. This
may result in explosion immediately there or elsewhere in network wherever explosive
mixture of air and hydrocarbon vapors is present in appropriate concentration for
explosion.
25.3. Surface Drain System (SS):
The peripheral open surface Drain is laid on east of Power Plant-I unit and the final
point is routing to either CRWS or to Storm Sewer System depending upon its oil
content. Normally surface drain outlets are kept floating on CRWS system. During
rains, the outlet is changed over to Storm water sewer after one hour to reduce the load
on ETP-I. SS open drain channel is covered by grills.
25.4. Contaminated Rain water system (CRWS):
Conceptually the rain water accumulated over the floor which will wash the oil and
other chemical contaminants present on the floor and pumps area, is collected through
catch basins provided at different locations and sent to WWTP in ETP-II. But in Power
Plant-I no such Contaminated Rain water system (CRWS) is provided as hydrocarbon
handling is less and draining arrangement is nil.

content:
26.0. Environmental Management:
Environmental Management is accorded prime importance during day to day operation
of the Refinery. The Refinery is an ISO certified installation. As per The Environmental
policy of Visakh Refinery, The Refinery is committed to conduct its operation in
compatible with environmental and economic development of the country & community.
Its aim is to protect, respect and strive towards improvement of the environment through
awareness and involvement of every employee by ensuring safe, healthy & environment
friendly work practices and operating procedures.
26.1. Liquid Effluent generation and control:

26.4. Procedure for Housekeeping:
a. Plant shift supervisor and technicians should prepare the list of places where
dirt/trash/scrap gets generated often. These are to be cleaned as and when CLW are
available. The status and CLW engagement is to be recorded in the allotted register.
Waste log form is to be filled for huge amount of waste for disposal. Plant shift
supervisor will ensure that process area, control room and other areas are cleaned
religiously and things are kept orderly. Every week consolidated list of pending items
are to be sent to section head. He will develop action plan to eliminate or minimize
the waste generation. The advantages/benefits of good housekeeping will be
disseminated repetitively (once in two months) to all operating personnel by the
section head.
b. Oil soaked rags and waste may ignite spontaneously. They must therefore be
discharged into metal containers specially marked and located for this purpose. Plant
shift supervisor should ensure covered metal drums are available and the stipulated
procedure id followed.
c. Lube oil drums (full and empty), toxic/nontoxic chemicals drums (full and empty),
ferrous/nonferrous scrap are to be stored in designated places.
d. All lube oil filled drums be stacked in upright position un a designated area. However
for drawing smaller quantities of lube oil for the requirement of pumps and motor
bearings, drums can be kept standing position on the suitable stand with valve
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:26 Rev. No.2 Page 221 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 26.0 Environmental Management: > 26.4 Procedure for Housekeeping:

content:
arrangement. Drip tray should be provided at the underneath of the drum valve to
collect oil drippings.
e. Shift supervisor should ensure that the unit operating area floor should be clean and
dry without any oil patches. Floor washing should be done only by hot water provided
in all units in limited quantities to remove any oil film on the floor surface. Fire
fighting equipment is for fire fighting only. Never use it for utility purpose nor
remove it for proper locations. However, if any oil spillage is occurred, fire water
(first aid fire hose) can be used to push the spilled oil into nearest OWS with bare
minimum quantity and it should be entered in TOB. Heavy oils like LSHS should be
removed manually into drums.
f. Shift supervisor should ensure that all full DCP cylinders are kept in the designated
boxes and first aid hose reels are rolled and secured on rack when not in use, if hose
is to be used for extended periods, string it above walkways to eliminate tripping
hazards. He also should ensure that the surrounding area of safety shower is clean
and free of debris/obstacles. The location of nearest safety shower should be
displayed in all strategic locations of the plant for easy identification of safety
showers in case of emergency. Fire monitor discharge capacity shall be written on
the monitor. Approach platforms of fire hydrant/ fire monitor and fire water
sprinklers isolation valves should be clean and free of debris.
g. All steam trap outlets should be connected either to condensate recovery system or it
should be extended to the nearest open drain or storm water drain. Extended lines
should be buried and should not cause tripping hazard. Copper tubing outlets should
be pointed upwards with the tapering end.
h. All equipment, vessel, process and utility pipe lines should be painted and numbered
as per the recommended colour code and numbering. Equipment numbering should
be done as per the P&ID and it should indicate the unit code, equipment legend (V-
Vessel, E-exchanger, T-Tank etc.,), serial number and service of the equipment. The
number size should be uniform throughout the unit. Normally the recommended size
to be followed is 3*3”.
i. All wheel spanners should be kept in wheel spanner stand located at the unit operating
area and in field room. All instrument and control valve tag no’s should be stenciled.

content:
27.1. Safe Work Practices:
Safety of personnel and equipment is very important. Ignorance of the details of the unit
or the techniques of safe and efficient operation reduces the margin of safety of personnel
and subjects the equipment to more hazardous conditions. All the operating and
maintenance crew therefore must be fully familiar with the equipment and materials
being handled in the unit, and recognize the hazards involved in handling them and the
measures taken to ensure safe operations. The unit handles with one of the potential
source of fire and explosion, Fuel oil, Fuel gas and chemical like Eloguard & Hydrazine,
therefore adherence of safety rules should be given uphill importance.
27.2. List of safe work practices:
The list of safe work practices in the refinery is given blow below
Sl. Title of Safe Work Practices (SWP) Availability
1. SWP-01 Specification of Personal Protective Equipment
1. Soft copies
2. SWP-02 Minimum Personal Protective Equipment for various
available in
activities Intranet.

6. SWP-06 Contractor job equipment safety assurance
7. SWP-07 Operations safety huddle
8. SWP-08 Control and Monitoring of Slippery Areas in Plants
9. SWP-09 Procedure for effective management of near misses
10. SWP-10 Lock out and tag out system for electrical isolations

27.3. Work Permit Procedure:
Please refer Work Permit Manual released by F&S dept. which is available on HPCL
VR intranet. The path to go the manual is as follows.
VR Home page  Department  Fire and Safety  Other Manuals  Revised Work
Permit system  Work Permit Manual 2014.
A PDF file will be opened on clicking the above path. Complete details on work permits
will be available in this file with respect to various types of permits and the escalation
matrix. However brief information is mentioned below for immediate reference.
27.4. Safe work practice (SWP) for Confined Space Entry:
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:27 Rev. No.2 Page 225 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 9 SWP-09 Procedure for effective management of near misses > 27.4 Safe work practice (SWP) for Confined Space Entry:

content:
It is the Permit for an activity within an enclosure with known or potential hazards and
restricted means of entrance and exit which is not normally occupied by people and is
usually not well ventilated.
Examples: Boilers Process Vessels (above ground & underground), Furnaces, Storage
tanks, Large Diameter pipes, OWS manholes, Effluent Switch pits, Chemical storage
room shed, Chemical / Process Pump Houses, Entering Excavation more than 1.2 meter
deep etc
27.3.1.Preparatory work before issuing Confined space entry Permit:

All personnel involved with confined space entry should be aware of hazards and no
person should enter the confined space unless entry permit has been obtained by the
concerned supervisor.

All the connected lines to the confined space have to blinded or disconnected by blind
flanges. No entry can be given without positive isolation.
 Power driven internal equipment such as mixers shall be disconnected electrically by
an authorized person.

Mechanical ventilation equipment shall be properly grounded (earthed) to dissipate
any static charges. Pneumatic air movers (educators) and exhaust fans are
recommended for this purpose. Electrical powered fans if any shall be explosion proof
types. Air intake of forced ventilation equipment shall be from an uFCCU-1
COntaminated location.

Utility or instrument air cannot be used as means of ventilation or as air supply to
breathing apparatus. All entrants shall wear personal monitors as required (H2S, CO,
O2).

Adequate illumination shall be provided using 24V or below explosion proof lamps.
Use of any electrical equipment above 24V shall have ‘earth leakage circuit breaker’
and also F&S department approval.

Wherever possible, the confined space shall be adequately ventilated to enable entry
without requirement of respiratory protection equipment. At least two man ways
should be open in every chamber of vessel for proper ventilation.
 Care should be taken when ventilating vessels containing ‘pyrophoric iron scales to
avoid spontaneous ignition. In such situation entry with required safety precautions or
handling pyrophoric iron in wet condition should be adopted.
 The confined area shall be made safe for entry by such methods as depressurizing,
venting, draining, steaming, washing and ventilating. Radiation sources if any shall
be removed.
 Shift in charge should be aware of possible behavioural effects of hazard exposure in
confined space area before issuing the work permit at confined space area.
27.3.2.Gas Testing Procedure:

Shift in charge has to ensure all the meters/instruments to be used for gas test are ready
to use.

Technicians have to ensure that the meter is in good condition and it has to be checked
and logged in TOB at the starting of the shift. If any problem is found with the
instruments, it has to be brought immediately to shift in charge’s notice.
 The gas tests have to be carried out and the testing result values have to be recorded
in the permit. F&S personnel can be called for entry into critical areas for witnessing
gas test and providing guidance as required.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:27 Rev. No.2 Page 226 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 9 SWP-09 Procedure for effective management of near misses > 27.4 Safe work practice (SWP) for Confined Space Entry:

content:

First, the meter should be checked in fresh air (preferably in safe zone/field room).
Batteries should not be changed or charged in hazardous area. Testing immediately
after stopping the purge may give misleading results. Purge medium or air mover
should be stopped temporarily for 15 minute or more depending on the size of space
prior to measurement.
 Initially the test has to be done from outside the confined space, using a long probe. If
the initial gas tests indicate a concentration above the permissible limits, further was
freeing should be done until the gas concentration is within permissible limits.

Test result should be representative of the entire confined space. Hence the need may
arise to enter for gas testing at different locations inside large tanks or towers and
complex vessels based upon number of man ways, toxic material handled, size of
vessel, etc to get a representative result. Operation shift supervisor should decide at
initial stage, the need for entering the confined space for gas testing.

The job executing supervisor should witness or satisfy himself, that actual gas test has
been done before issuing and accepting the permit. Receiver can refuse the permit if
proper gas test was not done. For critical entries, the receiver should accompany the
gas tester up to the man way.

At least 13% oxygen is required to obtain an accurate LEL reading from a combustible
gas meter. Hence, these cannot give a proper reading in atmospheres such as vessel
purged with steam/nitrogen. Hence LEL and O2 shall be measured simultaneously.

Gas meters should be calibrated once in a month and certified by HPCL
instrumentation section. Record for each gas tester is maintained by instrumentation
section. Next calibration due date should be marked on the tester.
27.3.3.Confined Space entry Permit:
 The permit receiver should give “safety pep-talk” to entrants about the hazards and
precautions prior to entry.

The receiver has to provide means for easy exit and entry of personnel into and out of
the confined space. Safety rope should be provided where required.

Hot work permit has to be issued in case of confined space entry and should be
completely filled. Non-relevant items should be struck off.
 The issuer has to make the receiver aware of the hazards involved in confined space
entry and precautions to be taken.

Where atmosphere within confined space is initially safe, but there is a reason to
believe that it may become unsafe during the period for which entry is authorized
(from emission of fumes from sludge or deposits contained in the space or welding
fumes), continuous gas monitoring is required. The same has to be mentioned on the
permit at the time of issuing.
27.3.4.Communication at Confined Area:
 Shift in charge should make effective communication visually or by voice with the
entrants, effective means of communication should be available. Intrinsically safe
hands-free communication sets should be preferred.

In IDLH (immediately dangerous to life or health) atmosphere, a common
communication link system should be provided and used by personnel who are
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:27 Rev. No.2 Page 227 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 9 SWP-09 Procedure for effective management of near misses > 27.4 Safe work practice (SWP) for Confined Space Entry:

content:
entering, standby, and maintaining the life support system. IDLH values for toxic
gases are H2S =300 ppm and CO= 1500 ppm.

Shift in charge should ensure that all the entrants should be evacuated if the
communication is interrupted.

Shift in charge may enter a confined space to attempt a rescue with the help of F&S
personnel and co-ordinate for rescue operations.

Shift in charge should monitor all the activities inside a confined space to determine
if it is safe for entrants to remain in the space and can order all the entrants to evacuate
in case he feels the presence of any prohibited condition.

F & S personnel have to be communicated as soon as the shift in charge determines
that authorized entrants may need assistance to escape from confined space hazards.
27.3.5.Requirements for Entry with Respiratory Protection:
 Entrants should wear air supplied respirators in oxygen deficient atmospheres, when
toxics are beyond TLV and where atmosphere within confined space is initially made
safe, but there is a reason to believe that it may become unsafe during the period for
which entry has been authorized.
 Under no circumstances chemical cartridge/canister type gas masks should be used
for confined space entry. Particular respirators have to be used if required.

Receivers have to ensure that air supplied respirators are in good condition, well
maintained and inspected according to manufacturer’s specifications.

Breathing apparatus users should be medically certified and properly trained to use
breathing apparatus.
 Entrants should wear harness attached to lifelines and be attended by an attendant who
should have breathing apparatus and clothing ready for use outside the confined space.

In IDLH atmospheres, entrants shall wear breathing apparatus or airline mask attached
with standby escape set. Rescue arrangements should be made ready with the help of
F&S.
27.5. Safe work practice (SWP) for Opening of Process Equipment & Piping:
In the refinery any maintenance job needs a preparatory work prior to conducting the job.
When any jobs (cold or hot) has to be done in areas where flammable/hazardous mixtures
are likely to be present, the isolation of equipment involved in the job, has to be done in
a proper and scientific way for the safety of the equipment and personnel.
27.4.1.Purging/ Isolation of the Equipment:
Before any maintenance job is done in the plant, it is necessary to remove all flammable
or toxic gases from the system. Inert gas, nitrogen or steam may be used for purging.
Water is used for flushing after ensuring that the equipment can carry weight of water
involved. The part of the plant or the system to be purged is to be isolated either by
closing appropriate valves or by proper blinding. No valves should be considered leak
proof and in any the cases of maintenance jobs. Isolation of equipment is to be done by
blinding. A blind list should be prepared for isolation of equipment and check shall be
done to comply with it.
If the line to be welded has contained flammable material it must be drained and section
should be disconnected at both ends. The whole section or the isolated portion should be
then gas freed and tested before welding commences. Where isolation and gas freeing
are impracticable, the line after draining should be washed thoroughly with water and
welding/hot job while charged with low-pressure steam or filled with water.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:27 Rev. No.2 Page 228 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 9 SWP-09 Procedure for effective management of near misses > 27.5 Safe work practice (SWP) for Opening of Process Equipment & Piping:

content:
While opening any flange joint for installation of blind, the following procedure must be
followed:

Wear protective clothing and appropriate eye/face protection.

Ensure adequate working platform is provided.

Initially flange bolts to be slackened slowly by standing upwind direction and cross
check for presence of flammable or toxic gas, blots opening can be performed at a
faster rate.

Removing flange bolts leaving a minimum of two bolts, then loose last two bolts
without completely removing the nuts. Spread the flanges to install blinds. Always
open flange on the side away from the person so that any sudden release will be
directed away from the personnel.

The presence of concerned operation technician is a must for any emergency help, like
further isolation etc.

Hot cutting for flange nut bolts will be permitted only when presence of flammable of
toxic gases is ensured zero.
27.6. Mud Packing:
Most common method used for plugging the end of pipeline for hot work is “MUD
PACKING”. The main objective of mud packing is to block the hydrocarbon vapour,
which might be present in the pipeline.
Typical sketch of mud packing:
Procedure for Mud Packing:

Cold cut the line on which the flange is to be welded.

Insert the plug into the pipe as shown in the sketch.
 The plug must have a hollow 1” pipe connected at the center and pipe will be
extended to a distance 5 feet away from the hot work area. The purpose of hollow
pipe at the center is to prevent any pressure build-up on the upstream. At frequent
intervals the presence of toxic/flammable gases is to be checked and recorded so as
to ensure that there is no abnormal situation at the site. The gap between the pipe
and plug shall be nicely packed with clay mud as shown in the sketch.
 The gas coming out from the vent point to be continuously monitored.
 Once the welding is completed, the plug has to be taken out along with the mud by
pulling.
27.7. Procedure for Car Sealing of Critical Valves
 Critical Valves to be car sealed to avoid inadvertent operation of plant critical valves
due to human error to overcome plant shutdown/upsets & hazards and to indicate /
caution the mal-operation for eliminating the chances of potential near miss.
 Car sealed valves to be identified / recorded / checked by numbering.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:27 Rev. No.2 Page 229 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 9 SWP-09 Procedure for effective management of near misses > 27.7 Procedure for Car Sealing of Critical Valves

content:
 Car seals must be kept intact on the following critical valves for avoiding complete
plant shutdown / upsets / hazards.
 Unit Battery Limit Instrument Air header isolation valve: It should not be closed
without authorization as it will lead to complete plant shut down on instrument air
failure.
 Unit Battery Limit Bearing Cooling Supply header isolation valve: It should not be
closed without authorization as it will lead all running rotary equipment seizing &
complete plant upset.
 Unit Battery Limit Bearing Cooling Return header isolation valve: It should not be
closed without authorization as it will lead all running rotary equipment seizing
because of BCW flow stoppage to bearing due to back pressure & complete plant
upset.
 Unit Battery Limit Fire Water header isolation valve: It should not be closed without
authorization as it will stop fire water supply to all fire hydrants & FAHR which is
very essential for fire incidents & also plant will go complete shut down if fire is out
of control.
 Unit area First Aid Fire Hose Reels isolation valves: It’s should be operated only in
case of fire incidents & must be recorded to analyse the root cause of the incident.
Again it must not be operated for other purpose as salt water to ETP will cause Bio
system degradation.
 The Following two registers should be maintained as the formats given below:

content:
28.0. Blind Lists, Vendor Manuals & Checklist:
Blinds are essential to get positive isolation of the equipment. All the connected lines of
equipment are to be isolated, depressured and blinded prior to inspection and
maintenance activity of the equipment. After completion of the inspection and
maintenance activity all the lines are to be deblinded as per list.
Vendor manuals are to be referred to get specific details like process conditions,
material specifications, component Nos, equipment engineering drawings,Vendor
contact details etc.
Checklists are to be followed in all applicable areas like startup, shutdown, PM checks,
Maintenance handing over, Maintenance taking over etc.

28.10.Annexures:
28.9.1.Annexure-I – FCCU-I & II CO Boilers Startup Check list (After Short
Shutdown)
Sl. Check list Point Remarks
1. Clearance obtained from YSF for boiler start up.
2. Walk around the boiler & check for any abnormality.
3. Ensure all the blinds provided are removed & man-ways
boxed up.
4. Ensure all the instruments & pressure switches are aligned.
5. Ensure Main stop valve is in closed position.
6. Ensure Desuperheater controls are in closed position.
7. Ensure the concerned boiler’s controls are on Manual in
MOI.
8. Ensure all the gauge glasses are in service with sufficient
lighting.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:28 Rev. No.2 Page 237 of 251
breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 6 3" 300# B2A > 28.9 Vendor Manuals:

content:
Sl. Check list Point Remarks
9. Ensure fuel oil recirculation is established.
10. Ensure Boiler outlet damper to stack is in open position.
11. Ensure good housekeeping & no lube oil leak around FD
fan turbine area.
12. Start AOP for FD fan turbine Lube oil circulation.
13. Start FD fan & adjust the air flow.
14. Ensure MOP cut in, then stop AOP & keep in auto mode.
15. Check auto cut in of FD fan from motor to turbine and
vice-versa
16. Ensure the power supply to BMS panel.
17. Ensure the purge interlocks are ok.
18. Then start furnace purging.
19. Ensure pilot burner interlocks are okay.
20. Ensure fuel gas is liquid free.
21. Ignite the pilot burner.
22. Check for the pilot flame.
23. Ensure the main burner interlocks are okay.
24. Close Fuel oil recirculation.
25. Flash the main fuel oil / fuel gas burner.
26. Check the main flame pattern & stack is clear.
27. Ensure drum vent, startup vent, Super heater I/L, O/L
drain are in open position.
28. Slowly increase the furnace temp & drum pressure as per
start up procedure
29. After reaching 2 kg/cm2 drum pressure , close drum vents
30. After reaching 150 C steam temperature , close S/H I/L
drains
31. Steadily raise steam pr. & temp. in 3 to 4hrs to working
parameter & in 1 hr for hot boiler
32. After reaching working pressure & temperature &
ensuring all trip checks ok ( after 72 hrs startup ) open
Main steam stop v/v after opening equalization v/v bring
boiler on range
33. Establish steady load on boiler (higher than minimum
load) & close start up vent & then super heater O/L drain.
34. Ensure all everything normal & then take all the controls
on auto at MOI.
35. Increase the chemical dosing pump stroke according to the
boiler load.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:28 Rev. No.2 Page 238 of 251

content:
WHRS Startup Check list
Sl. Check list Point Remarks
1. Clearance obtained from YSF for boiler start up.
2. Walk around the boiler & check for any abnormality.
3. Ensure all the blinds provided are removed & man-ways
boxed up.
4. Ensure all the instruments are aligned.
5. Ensure LP Steam Main stop valve is in closed position.
6. Ensure the concerned boiler’s controls are on Manual in
MOI.
7. Ensure all the gauge glasses are in service with sufficient
lighting.
8. Ensure Boiler outlet damper to FGD is in open position.
9. Ensure good housekeeping
10. Ensure drum vent, startup vent, LP Steam I/L, O/L drain are
in open position.
11. Slowly increase the furnace temp & drum pressure by
closing the OCO Boiler Stack Damper
12. After reaching 2 kg/cm2 Steam drum pressure ,close drum
vents
13. Steadily raise steam pr. & temp. in 3 to 4hrs to working
parameter & in 1 hr for hot boiler
14. After reaching working pressure & temperature ,open Main
steam stop v/v after opening equalization v/v bring boiler on
range
15. Establish steady load on boiler (higher than minimum load)
& close start up vent and MSL drain
16. Ensure all everything normal & then take all the controls on
auto at MOI.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:28 Rev. No.2 Page 239 of 251

Startup Clearance Format from Maintenance-Onsite:
HPCL VISAKH REFINERY - OPERATIONS DEPT. FORM :
OPRN / T&I / 03
START UP CLEARANCE Rev-0 Issued date
FROM MAINTENANCE ON-SITE / OFF SITE Dec., 2004
(This is suggested format. Respective functions are free to modify checklists to achieve
common objective of Safety) (To be given by UAT - On-site / Off-site Engineer of the Maint
Dept. at least 48 hrs. prior to startup)
To
Manager - Operations (P&U Block)
Based on the request for “ Startup clearance ” received from you on _________________ (date
& time), a detailed checkup has been carried out between _______________ hrs. and
_______________ hrs. and our broad observations are as under
Unit / Equipment: ______________ Date: __________ Time : __________
Sl. Item Description Yes No
1. Are all flanges that were opened during the shutdown properly made up
? Gasket sizes, ratings and materials are properly checked? Bolting
correct size and length and properly tightened?
2. Are all removed PSV’s reinstalled with proper Tag Nos.?
3. Are restriction orifices properly tagged / installed as per proper direction
of flow?
4. Are all the hydro testing of pipe lines / required equipment are
completed as per plan and deblinding done? Are all equipment boxed
up?
5. Oil soaked insulations in the unit are removed as per operations advise?
6. All Scaffolding material removed? No wooden planks? All maintenance
material, tools, drums, combustible material, insulating materials &
waste removed?
7. All maintenance material, shutdown equipment, tools, combustible
material, insulating materials & waste removed?
8. Floor and platforms at all levels on all vessels are cleaned, access clear,
tripping hazards cleared? Safe and unobstructed access to unit,
equipment and emergency equipment ensured?
9. Total Housekeeping job is completed?
10. Any other item/comment which has safety implication and precaution to be followed
before startup of the Unit?
1.
2.
Basis the above, there is no additional safety risk induced into the system from our functional
area and the unit start up activities can commence :
 Immediately
 After completing certain identified jobs ( basis the above check list ), recheck will be done
on.................. and then start up clearance will be sent.
UAT Maint. Member (On-site/Off-site) Manager Maint. (On-site/Off-site)

& time), a detailed checkup has been carried out between ______________________ hrs. and
_______________ hrs. and our broad observations are as under
Unit / Equipment: ______________ Date: __________ Time : __________
Sl. Item Description Yes No
1. Are all flanges that were opened during the shutdown properly mated?
· Gasket sized, ratings and materials correct? Bolting correct size and length?
Have all instruments been properly sealed, traced, insulated for the new project
etc.,?
2. Are all equipment legibly identified (for old & new units/equipment)?
3. Respective checklists of Inst, electrical, Rotary, civil (III-B-III-E) are filled
and attached ?
4. Floor and platforms cleaned, access clear, tripping hazards cleared?
5. Explosion proof quality of electric equipment/ fittings & instruments is
maintained?
6. All temporary electrical connections, hand lamps, etc removed?
7. All earthing / bonding of process equipment/ pipes / electric equipment &
switch gear properly in place?
8. Have all relevant gaskets / blinds and covers installed?
9. Have all electrical seals been properly installed and poured? Fireproofing in
place? House keeping completed at all the areas?
10. Any other item/comment which has safety implication and precaution to be followed
before startup of the Unit?
1.
2.

area and the unit start up activities can commence :
